output_codes:\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n        if (\n            os.environ.get(\"TORCHDYNAMO_DEBUG_FUNCTION\")\n            and os.environ.get(\"TORCHDYNAMO_DEBUG_FUNCTION\") != code.co_name\n        ):\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n        if code.co_name == \"<genexpr>\" and code.co_filename.endswith(\n            (\n                \"transformers/file_utils.py\",\n                \"transformers/utils/generic.py\",\n                \"diffusers/utils/outputs.py\",\n            )\n        ):\n            # not needed, but cleans up torchbench error stats\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n        if code.co_name == \"__setattr__\":\n            # setattr could be tricky to handle generally,\n            # but also not likely useful to compile- skip the whole frame\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n        if code.co_name == \"__init__\" and code.co_filename.startswith(\n            os.path.dirname(torch.optim.__file__)\n        ):\n            # optimizer support is still incomplete see\n            # test_state_dict in test/dynamo/test_optimizers.py\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n\n        # Check if the frame is generated by an exec builtin call\n        # TODO - Running exec generated frame seems propagates f_globals to the\n        # next frames.\n        if code.co_name == \"<module>\" and code.co_filename == \"<string>\":\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n\n        if (\n            code.co_name == \"<lambda>\"\n            and code.co_filename == \"<string>\"\n            and not bool(frame.f_builtins)\n        ):\n            # namedtuple subclass constructor. Empty builtins cause issue with\n            # len keyword in LIST_LEN guard.\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n\n        if is_generator(code):\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Attempt to trace generator\",\n                context=\"\",\n                explanation=\"Generators cannot be compiled directly with `torch.compile`.\",\n                hints=[\n                    \"Call a generator from inside of a non-generator Python function and \"\n                    \"compile that function instead.\",\n                    *graph_break_hints.FUNDAMENTAL,\n                ],\n            )\n\n        if not has_tensor_in_frame(frame):\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n\n        # skip tracing non-recursive disabled functions\n        # detect if the previous frame (non-convert_frame) is a non-recursive disable wrapper\n        prev_frame = sys._getframe()\n        while (\n            prev_frame\n            and \"torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py\" in prev_frame.f_code.co_filename\n        ):\n            prev_frame = prev_frame.f_back  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        if (\n            prev_frame\n            and prev_frame.f_code is decorators._nonrecursive_disable_wrapper_code\n        ):\n            return ConvertFrameReturn(apply_to_code=False)\n\n        global initial_global_state\n        initial_global_state = GlobalStateGuard()\n\n        compile_id = get_compile_id(frame_state)\n        frame_id = compile_id.frame_id\n\n        signpost_event(\n            \"dynamo\",\n            \"_convert_frame_assert._compile\",\n            {\n                \"co_name\": code.co_name,\n                \"frame_id\": frame_id,\n                \"compile_id\": str(compile_id),\n                \"co_filename\": code.co_filename,\n                \"co_firstlineno\": code.co_firstlineno,\n                \"cache_size\": cache_size.num_cache_entries_with_same_id_matched_objs,\n                \"accumulated_cache_size\": cache_size.num_cache_entries,\n            },\n        )\n\n        # Record traced frames, skipping Dynamo generated ones.\n        if not code.co_name.startswith(TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX):\n            info = f\"{code.co_name} {code.co_filename}:{code.co_firstlineno}\"\n            dynamo_tls.traced_frame_infos.append(info)\n\n        with compile_context(CompileContext(compile_id)):\n            result = _compile(\n                frame.f_code,\n                frame.f_globals,\n                frame.f_locals,\n                frame.f_builtins,\n                frame.closure,\n                self._torchdynamo_orig_backend,\n                self._one_graph,\n                self._export,\n                self._export_constraints,\n                hooks,\n                cache_entry,\n                cache_size,\n                frame,\n                frame_state=frame_state,\n                compile_id=compile_id,\n                skip=skip + 1,\n                package=self._package,\n                convert_frame_box=self._box,\n            )\n\n        if config.caching_precompile and self._package is not None:\n            from .package import DynamoCache\n\n            # Record that the dynamo package has changed\n            DynamoCache.record_package(self._package)\n        return result\n\n\ndef convert_frame_assert(\n    compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\n    one_graph: bool = True,\n    export: bool = False,\n    export_constraints: Optional[typing.Never] = None,\n    package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\n) -> ConvertFrameAssert:\n    \"\"\"Fully convert a frame into an FX graph, raising an exception if we fail.\"\"\"\n    return ConvertFrameAssert(\n        compiler_fn, one_graph, export, export_constraints, package\n    )\n\n\nfrom collections import OrderedDict\n\nfrom torch.utils.hooks import RemovableHandle\n\n\nif typing.TYPE_CHECKING:\n    from .output_graph import OutputGraph\n\n# we have to use `OrderedDict` to make `RemovableHandle` work.\n_bytecode_hooks: dict[int, BytecodeHook] = OrderedDict()\n\n\ndef register_bytecode_hook(hook: BytecodeHook) -> RemovableHandle:\n    \"\"\"Register hooks for bytecode generated by Dynamo. The hook can do some\n    logging, as well as return a new code object to be used. Please refer\n    to `BytecodeHook` for the hook signature.\n    \"\"\"\n    handle = RemovableHandle(_bytecode_hooks)\n    _bytecode_hooks[handle.id] = hook\n    return handle\n\n\ndef _compile(\n    code: CodeType,\n    globals: dict[str, object],\n    locals: dict[str, object],\n    builtins: dict[str, object],\n    closure: tuple[CellType],\n    compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\n    one_graph: bool,\n    export: bool,\n    export_constraints: Optional[typing.Never],\n    hooks: Hooks,\n    cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\n    cache_size: CacheSizeRelevantForFrame,\n    frame: Optional[DynamoFrameType] = None,\n    frame_state: Optional[dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]]] = None,\n    *,\n    compile_id: CompileId,\n    skip: int = 0,\n    package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\n    # Can be used to record things for the caller, both\n    # in the case of normal and exception code paths\n    convert_frame_box: Optional[ConvertFrameBox] = None,\n) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n    from torch._inductor.async_compile import async_compile_pool_manager\n    from torch.fx.experimental.validator import (\n        bisect,\n        BisectValidationException,\n        translation_validation_enabled,\n        ValidationException,\n    )\n\n    # Only nonlocal defs here please!\n    # Time spent compiling this frame before restarting or failing analysis\n    dynamo_time_before_restart: float = 0.0\n    output: Optional[OutputGraph] = None\n    tracer: Optional[InstructionTranslator] = None\n\n    tf_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode] = (\n        torch.overrides._get_current_function_mode_stack()\n    )\n\n    @preserve_global_state\n    def transform(\n        instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, object]\n    ) -> None:\n        nonlocal output\n        nonlocal tracer\n        speculation_log.restart()  # type: ignore[has-type]\n        exn_vt_stack = ExceptionStack()\n        tracer = InstructionTranslator(\n            instructions,\n            code,\n            locals,\n            globals,\n            builtins,\n            closure,\n            tf_mode_stack,\n            code_options,\n            compiler_fn,\n            one_graph,\n            export,\n            export_constraints,\n            frame_state=frame_state,\n            speculation_log=speculation_log,  # type: ignore[has-type]\n            exn_vt_stack=exn_vt_stack,\n            distributed_state=distributed_state,  # type: ignore[has-type]\n            package=package,\n        )\n\n        try:\n            tracer.output.mark_bytecode_tracing_start()\n            with tracing(tracer.output.tracing_context), tracer.set_current_tx():\n                tracer.run()\n        except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\n            speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\n            raise\n        except (\n            exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\n            exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\n            exc.SkipFrame,\n        ):\n            raise\n        except Exception:\n            if translation_validation_enabled():\n                bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\n            raise\n        finally:\n            tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\n\n        output = tracer.output\n        assert output is not None\n        assert output.output_instructions\n        instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\n        code_options.update(output.code_options)\n        propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\n        check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\n        instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\n\n    @compile_time_strobelight_meta(phase_name=\"compile_inner\")\n    def compile_inner(\n        code: CodeType,\n        one_graph: bool,\n        hooks: Hooks,\n        transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\n    ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n        with contextlib.ExitStack() as stack:\n            stack.enter_context(\n                torch._dynamo.callback_handler.install_callbacks(\n                    CallbackTrigger.DYNAMO, str(CompileContext.current_compile_id())\n                )\n            )\n            stack.enter_context(CompileTimeInstructionCounter.record())\n            return _compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks, transform)\n\n        return (\n            ConvertFrameReturn()\n        )  # dead, but see https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/7577\n\n    @maybe_cprofile\n    def _compile_inner(\n        code: CodeType,\n        one_graph: bool,\n        hooks: Hooks,\n        transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\n    ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n        nonlocal dynamo_time_before_restart\n        last_attempt_start_time = start_time = time.time()\n\n        def log_bytecode(\n            prefix: str, name: str, filename: str, line_no: int, code: CodeType\n        ) -> None:\n            if bytecode_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\n                bytecode_log.debug(\n                    format_bytecode(prefix, name, filename, line_no, code)\n                )\n\n        log_bytecode(\n            \"ORIGINAL BYTECODE\",\n            code.co_name,\n            code.co_filename,\n            code.co_firstlineno,\n            code,\n        )\n\n        out_code = None\n        for attempt in itertools.count():\n            CompileContext.get().attempt = attempt\n            try:\n                with dynamo_timed(\n                    f\"compile_attempt_{attempt}\", log_pt2_compile_event=True\n                ):\n                    out_code = transform_code_object(code, transform)\n                break\n            except exc.RestartAnalysis as e:\n                if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\n                    TensorifyState.clear()\n                log.info(\n                    \"Restarting analysis due to %s\",\n                    LazyString(format_traceback_short, e.__traceback__),\n                )\n                # If restart reason is None just log the type of the exception\n                restart_reasons.add(e.restart_reason or str(type(e)))\n                # We now have a new \"last attempt\", reset the clock\n                last_attempt_start_time = time.time()\n                if attempt > 100:\n                    unimplemented_v2(\n                        gb_type=\"Excessive RestartAnalysis() calls\",\n                        context=\"\",\n                        explanation=\"Dynamo attempted to trace the same frame 100+ times. \"\n                        \"Giving up on compiling as the compile time tradeoff is likely not \"\n                        \"worth the performance gain.\",\n                        hints=[],\n                    )\n            except exc.SkipFrame as e:\n                if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\n                    TensorifyState.clear()\n                log.debug(\n                    \"Skipping frame %s %s \\\n                    %s %s\",\n                    e,\n                    code.co_name,\n                    code.co_filename,\n                    code.co_firstlineno,\n                )\n                if one_graph or _is_error_on_graph_break(tracer):\n                    log.debug(\n                        \"No graph captured with one_graph=True or error_on_graph_break=True\"\n                    )\n                return ConvertFrameReturn()\n\n        assert distributed_state is None or distributed_state.all_states is not None, (  # type: ignore[has-type]\n            \"compiler collective wasn't run before compilation completed\"\n        )\n\n        assert out_code is not None\n        log_bytecode(\n            \"MODIFIED BYTECODE\",\n            code.co_name,\n            code.co_filename,\n            code.co_firstlineno,\n            out_code,\n        )\n\n        for idx, hook in enumerate(_bytecode_hooks.values()):\n            with dynamo_timed(f\"bytecode_hooks_{idx}\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\n                hook_output = hook(code, out_code)\n                if hook_output is not None:\n                    out_code = hook_output\n\n        orig_code_map[out_code] = code\n        output_codes.add(out_code)\n        dynamo_time_before_restart = last_attempt_start_time - start_time\n        assert output is not None\n\n        # Tests for new code objects.\n        # The rationale for these tests can be found in torch/csrc/dynamo/eval_frame.c\n        # Only test once the code object is created.\n        # They are not tested during runtime.\n\n        def count_args(code: CodeType) -> int:\n            import inspect\n\n            return (\n                code.co_argcount\n                + code.co_kwonlyargcount\n                + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARARGS)\n                + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARKEYWORDS)\n            )\n\n        assert out_code is not None\n\n        total_argcount_old = count_args(code)\n        total_argcount_new = count_args(out_code)\n        msg = \"arg mismatch: \"\n        msg += f\"old code object has args {code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]}, \"\n        msg += f\"new code object has args {out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]}\"\n        assert (\n            code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]\n            == out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]\n        ), msg\n\n        msg = \"free var mismatch: \"\n        msg += f\"old code object has free var {code.co_freevars}, \"\n        msg += f\"new code object has free var {out_code.co_freevars}\"\n        assert code.co_freevars == out_code.co_freevars, msg\n\n        msg = \"cell var mismatch: \"\n        msg += f\"old code object has cell var {code.co_cellvars}, \"\n        msg += f\"new code object has cell var {out_code.co_cellvars}\"\n        assert code.co_cellvars == out_code.co_cellvars, msg\n\n        # Skipping Dynamo on a frame without any extracted graph.\n        # This does not affect eager functionality. But this is necessary\n        # for export for cases where Dynamo-reconstructed bytecode can create\n        # new function frames, confusing export in thinking that there\n        # are extra graphs now.\n\n        if output.export and output.is_empty_graph():\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n\n        assert output.guards is not None\n        CleanupManager.instance[out_code] = output.cleanups\n        nonlocal cache_entry\n        with dynamo_timed(\"build_guards\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\n            check_fn = CheckFunctionManager(\n                code,\n                output,\n                cache_entry,\n                hooks.guard_fail_fn if hooks else None,\n                hooks.guard_filter_fn if hooks else None,\n                guards_serialization_mode=\"save\" if package else None,\n            )\n\n        if package is not None:\n            assert check_fn.guards_state is not None\n            package.add_guarded_code(check_fn.guards_state, out_code)\n            package.add_inlined_source(output.tracing_context.traced_code)\n\n        compile_id_str = str(compile_id) if compile_id is not None else \"Unknown\"\n        annotation_str = \"Torch-Compiled Region: \" + compile_id_str\n        guarded_code = GuardedCode(\n            out_code,\n            check_fn.guard_manager,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            compile_id,\n            annotation_str,\n        )\n\n        if not output.is_empty_graph() and hooks.guard_export_fn is not None:\n            # We should not run the guard_export_fn when Dynamo does not\n            # generate any graph. This can happen in export when TorchDynamo\n            # generated bytecode has some reconstruction logic for mutated\n            # variables which can trigger TorchDynamo on the children frames but\n            # they are benign and do not generate any new graphs.\n            hooks.guard_export_fn(output.guards)\n\n        return wrap_guarded_code(guarded_code)\n\n    metrics_context = get_metrics_context()\n    code_context = (\n        package.code_context(code) if package is not None else contextlib.nullcontext()\n    )\n    with (\n        _use_lazy_graph_module(config.use_lazy_graph_module),\n        compile_context(CompileContext(compile_id)),\n        async_compile_pool_manager(),\n        chromium_event_timed(\n            \"dynamo\", reset_event_log_on_exit=True, log_pt2_compile_event=True\n        ),\n        _WaitCounter(\"pytorch.wait_counter.entire_forward_compile\").guard(),\n        metrics_context,\n        dynamo_timed(\n            \"_compile.compile_inner\",\n            phase_name=\"entire_frame_compile\",\n            dynamo_compile_column_us=\"dynamo_cumulative_compile_time_us\",\n        ),\n        code_context,\n    ):\n        restart_reasons: set[str] = set()\n        # This is shared across restarts\n        speculation_log = SpeculationLog()\n        if compile_pg := get_compile_pg():\n            distributed_state = DistributedState(compile_pg, LocalState())\n        else:\n            distributed_state = None\n\n        # Check recompilations\n        recompile_reason: Optional[str] = None\n        if is_recompilation(cache_size) and frame:\n            reasons = get_and_maybe_log_recompilation_reasons(cache_entry, frame)\n            recompile_reason = (\n                \"Unable to find recompilation reasons\" if not reasons else reasons[0]\n            )\n        # Recheck for recompilation, for when inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is set to False\n        inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate = False\n        if not config.inline_inbuilt_nn_modules and frame:\n            inbuilt_nn_reasons = get_and_maybe_log_recompilation_reasons(\n                cache_entry, frame, skip_logging=True\n            )\n            inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason = (\n                None if not inbuilt_nn_reasons else inbuilt_nn_reasons[0]\n            )\n\n            if (\n                inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason is not None\n                and \"[inline-inbuilt-nn-modules-candidate]\"\n                in inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason\n            ):\n                inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate = True\n\n        # Set if the recompile is a candidate for inline_inbuilt_nn_modules\n        # regardless of whether inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is set or not\n        metrics_context.update_outer(\n            {\n                \"recompile_reason\": recompile_reason,\n                \"inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate\": inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate,\n            }\n        )\n\n        recompile_user_contexts = get_hook_for_recompile_user_context()\n        if recompile_user_contexts:\n            # cap each user context to N chars for data retention purposes. N=256\n            # is chosen to be large enough to capture the most important info.\n            user_contexts_msg = {\n                user_context()[:256] for user_context in recompile_user_contexts\n            }\n            metrics_context.set(\"recompile_user_contexts\", user_contexts_msg)\n\n        exceeded, limit_type = exceeds_recompile_limit(cache_size, compile_id)\n        if exceeded:\n\n            def format_func_info(code: CodeType) -> str:\n                return f\"'{code.co_name}' ({code.co_filename}:{code.co_firstlineno})\"\n\n            # NS: Don't add period at the end of string, as it'll be added to URL\n            # rendering it incorrect\n            log.warning(\n                \"torch._dynamo hit config.%s (%s)\\n\"\n                \"   function: %s\\n\"\n                \"   last reason: %s\\n\"\n                'To log all recompilation reasons, use TORCH_LOGS=\"recompiles\".\\n'\n                \"To diagnose recompilation issues, see %s\",\n                limit_type,\n                getattr(config, limit_type),\n                format_func_info(code),\n                recompile_reason,\n                troubleshooting_url,\n            )\n            if config.fail_on_recompile_limit_hit:\n                raise FailOnRecompileLimitHit(\n                    f\"{limit_type} reached, because fail_on_recompile_limit_hit = True this is a HARD failure\"\n                )\n            elif one_graph or _is_error_on_graph_break(tracer):\n                raise FailOnRecompileLimitHit(\n                    f\"{limit_type} reached with one_graph=True or error_on_graph_break=True. \"\n                    \"Excessive recompilations can degrade \"\n                    \"performance due to the compilation overhead of each recompilation. To monitor \"\n                    \"recompilations, enable TORCH_LOGS=recompiles. If recompilations are expected, consider \"\n                    \"increasing torch._dynamo.config.cache_size_limit to an appropriate value.\"\n                )\n            elif justknobs_check(\n                \"pytorch/compiler:skip_code_recursive_on_recompile_limit_hit\"\n            ):\n                raise RecompileLimitExceeded(f\"{limit_type} reached\")\n            else:\n                # do not recursively skip frames\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"Dynamo cache limit exceeded\",\n                    context=f\"Limit type: {limit_type}\",\n                    explanation=\"Dynamo attempted to recompile the code object too many times, \"\n                    f\"exceeding the {limit_type} cache size limit.\"\n                    \"Giving up on compiling as the compile time tradeoff is likely not \"\n                    \"worth the performance gain.\",\n                    hints=[],\n                )\n\n        log.debug(\n            \"torchdynamo start compiling %s %s:%s, stack (elided %s frames):\\n%s\",\n            code.co_name,\n            code.co_filename,\n            code.co_firstlineno,\n            skip + 2,\n            # -2: omit current frame, omit contextlib decorator\n            \"\".join(CapturedTraceback.extract(skip=2 + skip).format()),\n        )\n        # -4: -2 as above, plus trace_structured frames\n        #\n        # NB: the frame looks like this:\n        #\n        # # handled by skip argument\n        # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:1069 in catch_errors\n        # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:910 in _convert_frame\n        # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:464 in _convert_frame_assert\n        # torch/_utils_internal.py:70 in wrapper_function\n        #\n        # # 2 current frame and context lib\n        # env/lib/python3.10/contextlib.py:79 in inner\n        # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:776 in _compile\n        #\n        # # 2 extra here\n        # torch/_logging/_internal.py:1064 in trace_structured\n        # torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py:780 in <lambda>\n        stack_trace = log_dynamo_start(code, skip)\n        start_time_ns = time.time_ns()\n        fail_type: Optional[str] = None\n        fail_reason: Optional[str] = None\n        fail_user_frame_filename: Optional[str] = None\n        fail_user_frame_lineno: Optional[int] = None\n        torch._dynamo.utils.ReinplaceCounters.clear()\n        guarded_code = None\n        try:\n            guarded_code = compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks, transform)\n\n            # NB: We only put_code_state in success case.  Success case here\n            # does include graph breaks; specifically, if a graph break still\n            # resulted in a partially compiled graph, we WILL return here.  An\n            # Unsupported exception will only bubble to the top level if we\n            # are unable to compile the frame at all.  In this case, there's\n            # no point in uploading the code state, because we will always\n            # fail exactly the same way even without the update.  (It's useful\n            # to upload for graph break though, because this can prevent\n            # extra graph break compilations.)\n            put_code_state()\n            log_frame_dynamic_whitelist(code)\n\n            return guarded_code\n        except Exception as e:\n            # NB: e's msg is mutated here to add user stack, but we DON'T want\n            # that stack in the Scuba logged fail_reason. So we grab the fail\n            # info here and add it to the metrics context below.\n            fail_type = type(e).__qualname__\n            fail_reason = str(e)\n            exception_handler(e, code, frame, export=export)\n            # NB: this is the post-mutation exception\n            torch._logging.trace_structured(\n                \"artifact\",\n                metadata_fn=lambda: {\n                    \"name\": \"dynamo_error\",\n                    \"encoding\": \"string\",\n                },\n                payload_fn=lambda: traceback.format_exc(),\n            )\n            fail_user_frame_filename, fail_user_frame_lineno = exc.get_exc_message(\n                e, compile_id\n            )\n            if tracer and tracer.is_tracing_resume_prologue:\n                # Do not allow any errors to be suppressed if tracer is currently tracing\n                # through resume function.\n                raise ResumePrologueTracingError(\n                    \"Error while tracing through a Dynamo-generated resume function prologue. \"\n                    \"Errors are not allowed when tracing resume function prologues.\\n\"\n                    f\"{type(e).__qualname__}: {str(e)}\"\n                ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n            elif isinstance(\n                e,\n                (\n                    Unsupported,\n                    TorchRuntimeError,\n                    BackendCompilerFailed,\n                    AssertionError,\n                    ConstraintViolationError,\n                    GuardOnDataDependentSymNode,\n                    ValidationException,\n                    UncapturedHigherOrderOpError,\n                    BisectValidationException,\n                    ShortenTraceback,\n                    PackageError,\n                ),\n            ):\n                raise\n            else:\n                # Rewrap for clarity\n                raise InternalTorchDynamoError(\n                    f\"{type(e).__qualname__}: {str(e)}\"\n                ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n        finally:\n            # Previously it was somewhere else and so it was\n            # possible to accidentally not log at all.\n            record_filename = getattr(e, \"record_filename\", None)\n            code = Each given parametrize_fn should each have the signature\n    f(test, generic_cls, device_cls).\n\n    The test names will be a combination of the names produced by the parametrize_fns in\n    \"<new_name>_<old_name>\" order. This order is done to match intuition for constructed names\n    when composing multiple decorators; the names will be built in top to bottom order when stacking\n    parametrization decorators.\n\n    Args:\n        old_parametrize_fn (callable) - First parametrize_fn to compose.\n        new_parametrize_fn (callable) - Second parametrize_fn to compose.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def composite_fn(test, generic_cls, device_cls,\n                     old_parametrize_fn=old_parametrize_fn,\n                     new_parametrize_fn=new_parametrize_fn):\n        old_tests = list(old_parametrize_fn(test, generic_cls, device_cls))\n        for (old_test, old_test_name, old_param_kwargs, old_dec_fn) in old_tests:\n            for (new_test, new_test_name, new_param_kwargs, new_dec_fn) in \\\n                    new_parametrize_fn(old_test, generic_cls, device_cls):\n                redundant_params = set(old_param_kwargs.keys()).intersection(new_param_kwargs.keys())\n                if redundant_params:\n                    raise RuntimeError('Parametrization over the same parameter by multiple parametrization '\n                                       f'decorators is not supported. For test \"{test.__name__}\", the following parameters '\n                                       f'are handled multiple times: {redundant_params}')\n                full_param_kwargs = {**old_param_kwargs, **new_param_kwargs}\n                merged_test_name = '{}{}{}'.format(new_test_name,\n                                                   '_' if old_test_name != '' and new_test_name != '' else '',\n                                                   old_test_name)\n\n                def merged_decorator_fn(param_kwargs, old_dec_fn=old_dec_fn, new_dec_fn=new_dec_fn):\n                    return list(old_dec_fn(param_kwargs)) + list(new_dec_fn(param_kwargs))\n\n                yield (new_test, merged_test_name, full_param_kwargs, merged_decorator_fn)\n\n    return composite_fn\n\n\ndef instantiate_parametrized_tests(generic_cls):\n    \"\"\"\n    Instantiates tests that have been decorated with a parametrize_fn. This is generally performed by a\n    decorator subclass of _TestParametrizer. The generic test will be replaced on the test class by\n    parametrized tests with specialized names. This should be used instead of\n    instantiate_device_type_tests() if the test class contains device-agnostic tests.\n\n    You can also use it as a class decorator. f(mod):\n            ret = []\n            for x in mod:\n                ret.append(x + 1)\n            return ret\n\n        mod = Mod()\n        counts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(f, backend=counts, fullgraph=True)\n        ref = f(mod)\n        res = opt_fn(mod)\n        res = opt_fn(mod)\n        res = opt_fn(mod)\n        res = opt_fn(mod)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n        self.assertEqual(counts.frame_count, 1)\n\n        mod.a.append(torch.randn(2, 2))\n        # `for x in mod` is inlined, where iter(m.a) creates a guard on the list length of m.a\n        # Mutating length of mod.a causes a re-compilation.\n        ref2 = f(mod)\n        res2 = opt_fn(mod)\n        res2 = opt_fn(mod)\n        res2 = opt_fn(mod)\n        res2 = opt_fn(mod)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref2, res2))\n        self.assertEqual(counts.frame_count, 2)\n\n    def test_compare_shapes_eq(self):\n        def compare_shapes(a, b, to_list):\n else:\n                return a + 2\n\n        # Test both ListVariable and ShapeVariable\n        torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(\n            self, lambda a, b: compare_shapes(a, b,        def __init__(self) -> None:\n                self.val = 0.5\n                self.count = 3\n\n        def fn(x, cfg):\n            for i in range(cfg.count):\n                x = x + cfg.val\n            return x\n\n        cfg1 = Cfg()\n        cfg1.val = 1.0\n        cfg2 = Cfg()\n        v = torch.zeros(1)\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.zeros(1)\n            input_keys = inputs_a.keys() | inputs_b.keys()\n            for k in input_keys:\n                if k in inputs_a:\n                    total += inputs_a[k]\n                if k in inputs_b:\n                    total += inputs_b[k]\n            return total\n\n        v1 = torch.Tensor([100])\n        v2 = torch.Tensor([200])\n        cnts = test_tensor_dict2(self):\n        def fn1(inputs):\n            total = torch.zeros(1)\n            for k, v in inputs.items():\n                total += v\n            return total\n\n        def fn2(inputs):\n            total = torch.zeros(1)\n            for v in inputs.values():\n                total += v\n            return total\n\n        def fn3(inputs):\n            total = torch.zeros(1)\n            for k in inputs.keys():\n                total += inputs[k]\n            return total\n\n        v1 = torch.Tensor([100])\n        v2 = torch.Tensor([200])\n        cnts = torch.compile(fn3, backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)\n        self.assertEqual(opt_fn1({\"a\": v1, \"b\": v2})[0], 300)\n        self.assertEqual(opt_fn2({\"a\": v1, \"b\": v2})[0], 300)\n        self.assertEqual(opt_fn3({\"a\": v1, \"b\": v2})[0], 300)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 3)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 9)\n\n    @patch.object(torch._dynamo.config, \"capture_scalar_outputs\", True)\n    def test_user_code_statically_known(self):\n        from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\n            has_static_value,\n            statically_known_true,\n        )\n\n        @torch.compile(fullgraph=True, backend=\"eager\")\n        def f(x):\n            # At this point, this isn't statically known, only the hint says so.\n            if statically_known_true(x.shape[0] > 9):\n                raise Exception()\n            torch._check(x.shape[0] >= 10)\n            # But now it is.\n            return statically_known_true(x.shape[0] > 9), has_static_value(x.shape[0])\n\n        x = torch.zeros(10)\n        torch._dynamo.mark_dynamic(x, 0)\n        self.assertEqual(f(x), (True, False))\n\n        @torch.compile(fullgraph=True, dynamic=True, backend=\"eager\")\n        def g(x, y):\n            n = x.item()\n            torch._check(n == 3)\n            return has_static_value(4.0), has_static_value(n)\n\n        out = g(torch.tensor([3]), torch.zeros(1))\n        self.assertEqual(out, (True, True))\n\n    def test_dictcomp(self):\n        def fn1(inputs):\n            return {k: backend=cnts)\n        self.assertEqual(opt_fn1({\"a\": v1, \"b\": v2})[\"a\"], 101)\n        self.assertEqual(opt_fn1({\"a\": v1, \"b\": v2})[\"b\"], 201)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 2)\n\n    def test_listcomp(self):\n        def fn2(inputs):\n            return torch.sum(torch.cat([v + 1 for k, v in inputs.items()], 0))\n\n        v1 = test_namedtuple_with_custom_getitem(self):\n        @torch.compile(fullgraph=True, backend=\"eager\")\n        def f(my_tuple):\n            return my_tuple.a + 1\n\n        class MyTuple(typing.NamedTuple):\n            a: torch.Tensor\n            b: torch.Tensor\n\n            def __getitem__(self, index):\n                return MyTuple(a[index], b[index])\n\n        a =           x = a\n            for i in rng:\n                x = x + i\n            return x\n\n        def fn1(a):\n            return fn(a, rng=range(3))\n\n        return a.shape[0]):\n                a += 1\n            return a\n\n        return torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(\n            self,\n            fn=fn,\n            nargs=1,\n            expected_ops=9,\n        )\n\n    def test_range_iter_guards(self):\n        @torch.compile()\n        def func():\n            @torch._dynamo.disable(recursive=False)\n            def run(n):\n                # For python <= 3.11, list comprehension is implemented by\n                # desugaring to:\n                # 1. creation of an iterator object\n                # 2. calling a new `listcomp` function with (1)\n                #\n                # In this test we force Dynamo to trace through (2) as the root\n                # frame, thereby ensuring we have the right guards for range\n                # iterators.\n                xs = [torch.ones(1) for i in range(n)]\n                return torch.concat(xs)\n\n            return run(2), run(3)\n\n        res2, res3 = func()\n        self.assertTrue(same(res2, torch.ones(2)))\n        self.assertTrue(same(res3, torch.ones(3)))\n\n    def test_range_iter_side_effects(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def run(x, it):\n            n = next(it)\n            return x + n\n\n        it = iter(range(1, 3))\n        res = run(torch.zeros(1), it)\n        self.assertTrue(same(res, torch.ones(1)))\n        self.assertEqual(next(it), 2)\n\n    def test_build_tuple_unpack(self):\n        def fn1(a, b, c):\n            return a - b / c\n\n        def fn2(a, b, c):\n            tmp1 = (a,)\n            tmp2 = (b, c)\n            args = (*tmp1, *tmp2)\n            return fn1(*args)\n\n        def fn3(a, *args):\n            return fn1(a, *args)\n\n        torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(self, fn=fn2, nargs=3, expected_ops=2)\n        torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(self, fn=fn3, nargs=3, expected_ops=2)\n\n    def test_list_mul(self):\n   )\n\n    def test_tuple_iadd_with_shape(self):\n        def fn(a):\n            output = (a + a.shape[0], a - a.shape[0])\n            # tuple += tuple\n            output += (a - a.shape[0], a + 2\n\n        class Foo:\n            field = Field()\n\n            def __init__(self):\n                self.__dict__[\"field\"] = 0\n\n        def fn(x, foo):\n            foo.field = 10\n self.custom_dict = {\"a\": torch.rand((2, 2))}\n                self.my_number = 42\n\n            def __getattribute__(self, name):\n                custom_dict = super().__getattribute__(\"custom_dict\")\n                if name in custom_dict:\n                    return custom_dict[name]\n                return super().__getattribute__(name)\n\n            def run(self, x):\n                return self.my_number * x + self.a * x\n\n        def fn(obj, x):\n            return obj.run(x)\n\n        obj = MyObject()\n        x = torch.rand((2, 2))\n     class MyMod(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super().__init__()\n                self.custom_dict = {\"queue\": [torch.rand((2, 2)) for _ in range(3)]}\n                self.other_attr = torch.rand((2, 2))\n\n            def __getattr__(self, name):\n                custom_dict = self.custom_dict\n                if name in custom_dict:\n                    return custom_dict[name]\n                return super().__getattr__(name)\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                return x @ self.other_attr + self.queue[-1]\n\n        x = torch.rand((2, 2))\n        mod = MyMod()\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_mod = torch.compile(mod, backend=cnts)\n        self.assertTrue(same(opt_mod(x), mod(x)))\n        self.assertTrue(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n        self.assertTrue(cnts.op_count, 2)\n\n    def test_nn_module_getattribute(self):\n        class MyMod(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super().__init__()\n                self.my_number = 42\n\n            def __getattribute__(self, name):\n                if name == \"special_attr\":\n                    return torch.tensor([[1, 2], [3, 4]])\n                return super().__getattribute__(name)\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                return self.my_number * x + self.special_attr * x\n\n        def fn(mod, x):\n            return mod(x)\n\n        mod = MyMod()\n        x = torch.rand((2, 2))\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n torch.Tensor\n            b: torch.Tensor = None\n            c: torch.Tensor = None\n            d: torch.Tensor = None\n            e: torch.Tensor = None\n\n        def fn(obj):\n            class_fields = dataclasses.fields(obj)\n            assert len(class_fields)\n            assert all(field.default is None for field in class_fields[1:])\n            other_fields_are_none = all(\n                getattr(obj, field.name) is None for field in class_fields[1:]\n            )\n            assert not other_fields_are_none\n\n            if not hasattr(obj, \"a\"):\n                return -1\n            if hasattr(obj, \"z\"):\n                return -2\n\n            total = getattr(obj, class_fields[0].name)\n            for field in class_fields[1:]:\n                v = getattr(obj, field.name)\n                if v is not None:\n                    total += v\n\n            return total\n\n        obj1 = MyDataClass(torch.randn(10), torch.randn(10), torch.randn(10))\n        obj2 = MyDataClass(torch.randn(10), e=torch.randn(10))\n        correct1 = fn(obj1)\n        correct2 = fn(obj2)\n\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n        self.assertTrue(same(opt_fn(obj1), correct1))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 2)\n\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n        self.assertTrue(same(opt_fn(obj2), correct2))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 1)\n\n        # guard failure\n        obj2.z = True\n        self.assertEqual(opt_fn(obj2), -2)\n\n    def test_dataclass_local_hasattr(self):\n        cnt = CompileCounter()\n        x = torch.randn(10)\n\n        @dataclasses.dataclass\n        class MyDataClass:\n            a: torch.Tensor\n            b: torch.Tensor\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=cnt, fullgraph=True)\n        def fn():\n            obj = MyDataClass(x + 1, x - 1)\n            if not hasattr(obj, \"a\"):\n                return -1\n            if hasattr(obj, \"z\"):\n                return -2\n            return obj\n\n        result = fn()\n        self.assertIsInstance(result, MyDataClass)\n        self.assertEqual(result.a, x + 1)\n        self.assertEqual(result.b, x - 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnt.frame_count, 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnt.op_count, 2)\n\n    def      samples = list(\n            sample_inputs_take_along_dim(\n                None, \"cpu\", torch.float32, requires_grad=False\n            )\n        )\n        cnts =  from torch._dynamo.variables.builtin import BuiltinVariable\n\n        operators = BuiltinVariable._fx_graph_functions()\n\n        for op, t1_np, t2_np in itertools.product(\n            operators, (True, False), (True, False)\n        ):\n            if op in [operator.eq, operator.ne]:\n                # returns equivalent of torch.eq/ne\n                continue\n            if op is operator.getitem:\n                # skip\n                # Did you know that tensor[ndarray_of_floats] works?\n                continue\n            if op is operator.imatmul and (t1_np or t2_np):\n                # skip\n                # in numpy, in place matmul does not work single\n                # dimensional arrays\n                continue\n            t1 = torch.rand(5)\n            if t1_np:\n                t1 = t1.numpy()\n            t2 = torch.rand(5)\n            if t2_np:\n                t2 = t2.numpy()\n            try:\n                # TODO try a bit harder\n                result = op(t1, t2)\n            except (RuntimeError, TypeError, IndexError):\n                continue\n            cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n            opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n            self.assertEqual(result, opt_fn(op, t1, t2), msg=f\"{op=} {t1_np=} {t2_np=}\")\n            self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1, msg=f\"{op=} {t1_np=} {t2_np=}\")\n            torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n    def test_numpy_ndarray_graph_break(self):\n        def fn(x):\n            a = x.numpy()\n            b = a.real\n format\n            test_cases = (\n                (3, 3),\n                (4, 4),\n                (5, 5),\n            )\n\n            for shape in test_cases:\n                t = torch.randn(shape, dtype=torch.complex64)\n                t_np = np.random.randn(*shape).astype(np.complex64)\n\n                _, t_np = _inf_nan_preprocess(t, t_np)\n                print(t, t_np)  # Just a side effect so that compilation kicks in\n\n        cnt = CompileCounterWithBackend(\"inductor\")\n        fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnt)\n        fn()\n        self.assertEqual(cnt.frame_count, ifdynstaticdefault(2, 1))\n\n    def test_mandelbrot_numpy(self):\n        def mandelbrot_numpy(max_iter):\n            # Define the boundaries of the complex plane\n            xn = 450\n            yn = 375\n            xmin = -2.25\n            xmax = 0.75\n            ymin = -1.25\n            ymax = 1.25\n\n            # Create the grid of complex numbers\n            x_values = np.linspace(xmin, xmax, xn, dtype=np.float64)\n            y_values = np.linspace(ymin, ymax, yn, dtype=np.float64)\n            rx, iy = np.meshgrid(x_values, y_values, indexing=\"xy\")\n\n            x = rx.copy()\n            y = iy.copy()\n            mask = np.zeros_like(x)\n            for i in range(max_iter):\n                x_prev = x\n                y_prev = y\n                x = x_prev**2 - y_prev**2 + rx\n                y = 2 * x_prev * y_prev + iy\n                inside = np.sqrt(x**2 + y**2) <= 2\n                mask += inside\n            return mask\n\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(mandelbrot_numpy, backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)\n        n_iter = torch._dynamo.config.recompile_limit - 2\n        for i in range(n_iter):\n            x = i + 3\n            ref = mandelbrot_numpy(x)\n            res = opt_fn(x)\n            self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n        # We need to specialise the number as it's in a forloop\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, n_iter)\n\n    def test_numpy_as_global(self):\n        global x\n        x = torch.rand(2, 3),\n            lambda x: x.transpose_(0, 1).mul_(2): torch.rand(2, 3),\n            lambda x: x.squeeze_().mul_(2): torch.rand(1, 2, 3),\n            lambda x: x.unsqueeze_(0).mul_(2): torch.rand(2, 3),\n            lambda x: x.resize_as_(torch.rand(200, 300)): torch.rand(2, 3),\n            lambda x: x.swapaxes_(0, 1).mul_(2): torch.rand(2, 3),\n            lambda x: x.swapdims_(0, 1).mul_(2): torch.rand(2, 3),\n            lambda x: x.rename_(\"N\", \"C\").mul_(2): torch.zeros(2, 3),\n            lambda x: x.as_strided_((3, 2), (2, 1)).mul_(2): torch.zeros(2, 3),\n            lambda x: x.detach_().mul_(2): torch.zeros(2, 3),\n        }\n        for func, args in func_args_map.items():\n            args_clone = args.clone()\n            cnts = self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n            self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 1)  # mul_\n\n    def test_out_variants_with_resizing_on_graph_inputs(self):\n        def fn(x, y):\n       self.assertTrue(same(fn(x, y), opt_fn(x.clone(), y.clone())))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n\n    def test_out_variants_with_resizing_on_graph_inputs_with_dynamic(self):\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/120482\n        class CustomModel(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super().__init__()\n\n            def forward(self, inputs):\n                return torch.outer(**inputs)\n\n        compile_fn = torch.compile(CustomModel(), backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n\n        shapes = [(2, 1), (6, 1), (4, 1)]\n        for shape in shapes:\n            vec1, vec2 = shape\n            input_tensor1 = torch.randn(vec1)\n            input_tensor2 = torch.randn(vec2)\n            out_tensor = torch.empty(shape)\n            args = {\"input\": input_tensor1, \"vec2\": input_tensor2, \"out\": out_tensor}\n            res = compile_fn(args)\n            opt_res = res.clone()  # cuz this is out and we mutate it\n            res = CustomModel()(args)\n            self.assertEqual(res, opt_res)\n\n    def test_out_variants_with_resizing_on_graph_inputs_with_dynamic1(self):\n        mv_op = torch.mv\n\n        def mv_out_op(a, b, c):\n            torch.mv(b, c, out=a)\n            return a\n\n        def fn(op, *args):\n            return op(*args)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n\n        ref = fn(mv_op, torch.ones(3, 3), torch.ones(3))\n        res = opt_fn(mv_op, torch.ones(3, 3), torch.ones(3))\n        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n\n        ref = fn(mv_out_op, torch.empty(0), torch.ones(3, 3), torch.ones(3))\n        res = opt_fn(mv_out_op, torch.empty(0), torch.ones(3, 3), torch.ones(3))\n        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n\n    def test_mutable_mapping_multiple_inheritance(self):\n        class MyWeirdDict(collections.abc.MutableMapping, torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self, **kwargs):\n                super().__init__()\n                self._items = kwargs\n\n            def keys(self):\n                return self._items.keys()\n\n            def __getitem__(self, item):\n                return self._items[item]\n\n            def __setitem__(self, key, value):\n                self._items[key] = value\n\n            def __delitem__(self, item):\n                del self._items[item]\n\n            def __len__(self):\n                return len(self._items)\n\n            def __iter__(self):\n                yield from self._items\n\n            def __hash__(self):\n                return hash(id(self))\n\n            def items(self):\n                for k, v in self._items.items():\n                    yield (k, v)\n\n        @torch.compile(fullgraph=True)\n        def to_weird_dict(td):\n            return MyWeirdDict(**td)\n\n        d = MyWeirdDict(a=1, b=2, c=3)\n        res = to_weird_dict(d)\n        self.assertEqual(tuple(d.items()), tuple(res.items()))\n\n    def test_dunder_new_function_inlining(self):\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/107460\n\n        counters.clear()\n\n        class ModelA(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super().__init__()\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                return torch.tanh(x + 1)\n\n        class ModelB(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __new__(cls):\n                return ModelA()\n\n        class Model(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super().__init__()\n                self.layer = torch.nn.Linear(2, 2)\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                other = ModelB()\n                return self.layer(x) + other(x)\n\n        x = torch.rand(2, 2)\n        m = Model()\n\n        opt_m = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)(m)\n        ref = m(x)\n        res = opt_m(x)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n    def 5\n\n            def run(self, x):\n                return x * self.c\n\n        def fn(x):\n            mock = Mock()\n            return mock.run(x)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn,   def fn(x):\n            ev = ElectricCar(\"Tesla\", \"Model S\", 2022, 4, \"100 kWh\")\n            return ev.run(x)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n\n        x = torch.randn(4)\n\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n\n    def test_dunder_new_function_inlining3(self):\n        class Foo:\n            def __new__(cls):\n                instance = return torch.sin(x) * self.a\n\n        class Bar:\n            def __new__(cls):\n                instance = object.__new__(Foo)  # not returning a new instance of Bar\n                instance.a = 7\n                return instance\n\n            def __init__(self):\n                self.a = 11  # not called in Bar()\n\n            def run(self, x):\n                return torch.sin(x) *        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n\n    def test_user_defined_object_class_interaction(self):\n        class Foo:\n            x = 5\n\n        class Mock:\n            # This is a class variable\n            class_variable = Foo()\n\n            @classmethod\n            def get_class_variable(cls):\n                # Accessing the class variable using the cls parameter\n                return Base1:\n            def __new__(cls):\n                return super().__new__(cls)\n\n            def __init__(self):\n                super().__init__()\n                if not hasattr(self, \"base2\"):\n                    raise ValueError(\"Wrong MRO tracing\")\n                self.base1 = 3\n\n        class Base2:\n            def __new__(cls):\n                return super().__new__(cls)\n\n            def __init__(self):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.base2 = 5\n\n        class Derived(Base1, Base2):\n            def __new__(cls):\n                return super().__new__(cls)\n\n            def __init__(self):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.derived = 7\n\n            def run(self, x):\n                return self.base1 * self.base2 * self.derived * x\n\n        def fn(x):\n            o = Derived()\n            return o.run(x)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        x = torch.randn(4)\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n\n    def test_class_duner_mro(self):\n        class ModuleA(torch.nn.Module):\n            pass\n\n        class ModuleB(ModuleA):\n            pass\n\n        def fn(x, mod):\n            if ModuleA in type(mod).__mro__:\n                return x + 1\n            else:\n                return x - 1\n\n        x = torch.rand(2, 3)\n        mod = ModuleB()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)(fn)\n        ref = fn(x, mod)\n        res = opt_fn(x, mod)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n    def test_class_duner_flags(self):\n        class ModuleA(torch.nn.ModuleDict, collections.abc.MutableMapping):\n            def __hash__(self):\n                return id(self)\n\n        def fn(x, ModuleA\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)(fn)\n        ref = fn(x, mod_class)\n        res = opt_fn(x, mod_class)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n test_nested_wraps(self):\n        def foo(x, y):\n            def add(x, y):\n                return x + y\n\n            @functools.wraps(add)\n            def wrapped_call(x, y):\n                return add(x, y)\n\n            return wrapped_call(x, y)\n\n        x = torch.randn(3, 3)\n        y = torch.randn(3, 3)\n\n        o = torch.compile(foo, fullgraph=True, backend=\"eager\")(x, y)\n        self.assertEqual(o, x + y)\n\n        def foo(x, y):\n            def nested_call(x, y):\n                def mul(x, y):\n                    return x * y\n\n                @functools.wraps(mul)\n                def double_nested_call(x, y):\n                    return mul(x, y)\n\n                return double_nested_call(x, y)\n\n            return nested_call(x, y)\n\n        o = torch.compile(foo, fullgraph=True, backend=\"eager\")(x, y)\n        self.assertEqual(o, x * y)\n\n    def test_module_deepcopy(self):\n        m1 = torch.nn.Sequential(\n            torch.nn.Linear(10, 10),\n            torch.nn.ReLU(),\n            torch.nn.Linear(10, 10),\n            torch.nn.ReLU(),\n        )\n        m2 = torch.nn.Sequential(\n            torch.nn.Linear(10, 10),\n            torch.nn.ReLU(),\n            torch.nn.Linear(10, 10),\n            torch.nn.ReLU(),\n        )\n\n        def fn(m, x):\n            m_copy = copy.deepcopy(m)\n            return m_copy(x)\n\n        v = torch.randn(10)\n        correct1 = fn(m1, v)\n        correct2 = fn(m2, v)\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n        for _ in range(10):\n            self.assertTrue(same(opt_fn(m1, v), correct1))\n        for _ in range(10):\n            self.assertTrue(same(opt_fn(m2, v), correct2))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 4)\n\n    def test_global_state_guard_serialization(self):\n        GlobalStateGuard = torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard\n        guards = GlobalStateGuard()\n        serialized_guards = guards.__getstate__()\n        json_guards = json.loads(serialized_guards)\n\n        samples = []\n        # Test on non autocast state and autocast cache states.\n        self.assertIn(\"autocast_state\", json_guards)\n        for key, value in json_guards.items():\n            if type(value) == int:\n                variant = value + 1\n            elif type(value) == bool:\n                variant = not value\n            elif isinstance(value, dict) and key == \"autocast_state\":\n                variant = value.copy()\n                variant[\"cached_enabled\"] = not variant[\"cached_enabled\"]\n                continue\n            else:\n                self.fail(f\"Unknown global state type {key}: {value}\")\n            new_dict = json_guards.copy()\n            new_dict[key] = variant\n            samples.append(new_dict)\n\n        for sample in samples:\n            guards.__setstate__(json.dumps(sample))\n            self.assertFalse(guards.check())\n\n        guards.__setstate__(json.dumps(json_guards))\n        self.assertTrue(guards.check())\n\n        # Test on autocast states.\n        def _test_autocast(dtype):\n            with torch.autocast(\"cpu\", dtype):\n                guards = GlobalStateGuard()\n                serialized_guards = guards.__getstate__()\n                json_guards = json.loads(serialized_guards)\n\n                for i, enabled in enumerate(json_guards[\"autocast_state\"][\"enabled\"]):\n                    if enabled:\n                        self.assertEqual(\n                            type(json_guards[\"autocast_state\"][\"dtype\"][i]), int\n                        )\n                        json_guards[\"autocast_state\"][\"dtype\"][i] += 1\n                        guards.__setstate__(json.dumps(json_guards))\n                        self.assertFalse(guards.check())\n\n        _test_autocast(torch.float16)\n        _test_autocast(torch.float32)\n        _test_autocast(torch.float64)\n        _test_autocast(torch.bfloat16)\n\n    def test_type_copy(self):\n        def fn(seq):\n            a, b = seq\n            return type(seq)([a + 1, b + 2, a + b])\n\n        args1 = [torch.randn(10), torch.randn(10)]\n        args2 = (torch.randn(10), torch.randn(10))\n        correct1 = fn(args1)\n        correct2 = fn(args2)\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n        self.assertTrue(same(opt_fn(args1), correct1))\n        self.assertTrue(same(opt_fn(args2), correct2))\n        self.assertIsInstance(opt_fn(args1), list)\n        self.assertIsInstance(opt_fn(args2), tuple)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 6)\n\n    def test_setattr_mutation1(self):\n        class MyObj:  # noqa: B903\n            def __init__(self, a, b):\n                self.a = a\n                self.b = b\n\n        def fn(obj):\n            obj.c = obj.a * obj.b + 1\n            obj.b = obj.a * obj.c + 2\n            obj.a = obj.b * obj.c + 3\n            obj.c = obj.a * obj.b + 4\n            obj.b = obj.a * obj.c + 5\n            obj.a = obj.b * obj.c + 6\n            return obj\n\n        x1 = torch.randn(10)\n        x2 = torch.randn(10)\n        obj1 = MyObj(x1, x2)\n        obj2 =       return x + 2\n            else:\n                return x + 3\n\n        x = torch.rand(3)\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        for c in [MyClass1, MyClass2]:\n            ref = fn(x, c)\n            res = opt_fn(x, c)\n            self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n    def test_super_calling_with_metaclass(self):\n        class ExampleMeta(type):\n            pass\n\n        class MyClass1(metaclass=ExampleMeta):\n            coeff = 4  # Force the constant guard to test source in guards\n\n            @classmethod\n            def add(cls, x):\n                return x + 1\n\n        class MyClass2(MyClass1):\n            @classmethod\n            def add(cls, x):\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                return x + super().add(x) + super().coeff\n\n        def fn(x, obj):\n            return x + obj.add(x)\n\n        x = torch.rand(3)\n        obj = MyClass2()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        ref = fn(x, obj)\n        res = opt_fn(x, obj)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n    def test_usr_cls_staticmethod(self):\n        class Foo:\n            @staticmethod\n            def bar(a, b):\n                return a + b\n\n        def fn(a, b):\n            return Foo.bar(a, b) - 1\n\n        torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(self, fn=fn, nargs=2)\n\n    def test_usr_cls_classmethod(self):\n        class Foo:\n            @classmethod\n            def bar(cls, a, b):\n                return a + b\n\n        def fn(a, b):\n            return Foo.bar(a, b) - 1\n\n        torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(self, fn=fn, nargs=2)\n\n    def test_dunder_methods(self):\n        class Foo:\n            def __init__(self, val):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.val = val\n\n            def __add__(self, other):\n                return Foo(self.val + other.val)\n\n            def __mul__(self, other):\n                return Foo(self.val * other.val)\n\n            def __truediv__(self, other):\n                return Foo(self.val / other.val)\n\n            def __sub__(self, other):\n                return Foo(self.val - other.val)\n\n        def fn(a, b, c):\n            return Foo(a) + Foo(b) * Foo(c) / Foo(a) - Foo(b)\n\n        torch._dynamo.testing.standard_test(self, fn=fn, nargs=3, expected_ops=4)\n\n    def test_function_annotation(self):\n        class Variable:\n            pass\n\n        def fn(x):\n            x = x / 3.0\n\n            def inner(y: typing.List[Variable]):\n                return x + 1\n\n            return inner\n\n        x1 = torch.randn(10)\n        obj2 = fn(x1)([])\n\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch._dynamo.optimize_assert(cnts)(fn)\n        opt_fn_inner = torch._dynamo.optimize_assert(cnts)(opt_fn(x1))\n        obj1 = opt_fn_inner([])\n        self.assertTrue(same(obj1, obj2))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 2)\n\n    def test_function_generic_alias_annotation(self):\n        class Variable:\n            pass\n\n        def fn(x):\n            x = x / 3.0\n\n            def inner(y: list[Variable]):\n                return x + 1\n\n            return inner\n\n        x1 = torch.randn(10)\n        obj2 **kwargs):\n                assert len(args) == 1\n                assert len(kwargs) == 1\n                v2 = torch.randn(10) + args[0] + kwargs[\"b\"]\n\n                def fn3(v3=torch.randn(10)):\n                    def fn4():\n                        return v0 + v1 + v2 + v3 + 1\n\n                    return fn4\n\n                return fn3\n\n            return fn2(1, b=2)()\n\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn1 = torch._dynamo.optimize_assert(cnts)(fn1)\n        tmp1 = torch._dynamo.optimize_assert(cnts)(opt_fn1())\n        tmp2 = torch._dynamo.optimize_assert(cnts)(opt_fn1())\n        self.assertTrue(tmp1().shape, (10,))\n        self.assertTrue(same(tmp1(), tmp1()))\n        self.assertFalse(same(tmp1(), tmp2()))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 9)\n\n    def test_nested_closure_mutation(self):\n        def fn1():\n            v1 = torch.randn(10)\n\n            def fn2():\n                v2 = torch.randn(10)\n\n                def fn3():\n                    nonlocal v1, v2\n                    v1 += 1\n                    v2 += 2\n                    return v1 + v2\n\n                return fn3\n\n            rv = fn2()\n            rv()\n            rv()\n            return rv\n\n        torch.manual_seed(9000)\n        counter1 = fn1()\n        result1 = [counter1(), counter1(), counter1()]\n\n        torch.manual_seed(9000)\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn1 = torch._dynamo.optimize_assert(cnts)(fn1)\n        counter2 = torch._dynamo.optimize_assert(cnts)(opt_fn1())\n        result2 = [counter2(), counter2(), counter2()]\n        result1.append(counter1())\n        result2.append(counter2())\n\n        self.assertTrue(same(result1, result2))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 11)\n\n    def test_write_to_closures_in_inlining(self):\n        out = []\n        for use_dynamo in [False, True]:\n\n            def make_counter():\n                x = torch.randn(10)\n\n                def counter():\n                    nonlocal x\n                    x = x + 1\n                    return x\n\n                return counter\n\n            torch.manual_seed(0)\n            counter = make_counter()\n            if not use_dynamo:\n                out.append(counter() + counter())\n            else:\n                cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n\n                @torch.compile(backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)\n                def fn(counter):\n                    return counter() + counter()\n\n                out.append(fn(counter))\n                self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n                self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 3)\n                self.assertFalse(same(counter() + counter(), out[-1]))\n\n        self.assertTrue(same(out[0], out[1]))\n\n    # When we unspecialize float, we wobble this test by changing\n    # the op count since previously we would just specialize and constant\n    # fold floats into the graph, whereas when we unspecialize we will have\n    # ops for item, add, and all other tensorified operations. Since this\n    # test really isn't testing that, we purposely specialize floats here.\n    @torch._dynamo.config.patch(specialize_float=True)\n    def test_closure_out_of_scope_cell(self):\n        cell1 = torch.rand(1).item()\n        cell2 = torch.rand(3, 3)\n\n        def indirect():\n            return direct()\n\n        def direct():\n            def inner():\n                return cell1 + 1, cell2 + 3\n\n            return inner()\n\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(indirect, backend=cnts)\n        result1, result2 = opt_fn()\n        self.assertAlmostEqual(cell1 + 1, result1)\n        self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(cell2 + 3, result2))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 1)\n\n    # When we unspecialize float, we wobble this test by changing\n    # the op count since previously we would just specialize and constant\n    # fold floats into the graph, whereas when we unspecialize we will have\n    # ops for item, add, and all other tensorified operations. Since this\n    # test really isn't testing that, we purposely specialize floats here.\n    @torch._dynamo.config.patch(specialize_float=True)\n    def test_closure_out_of_scope_cell_with_mutation(self):\n        cell1 = torch.rand(1).item()\n        orig1 = cell1\n        cell2 = torch.rand(3, 3)\n        orig2 = cell2.clone()\n\n        def indirect():\n            return direct()\n\n        def direct():\n            def inner():\n                nonlocal cell1, cell2\n                x = cell2 + 1\n                cell1 += 1\n                cell2 += 10\n                x = x + cell2\n                return cell1, cell2, x\n\n            return inner()\n\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(indirect, backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)\n        for i in range(1, 4):\n            result1, result2, _ = opt_fn()\n            self.assertAlmostEqual(orig1 + 1 * i, result1)\n            self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(orig2 + 10 * i, result2))\n            self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n            self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 3)\n            cnts.clear()\n\n    def test_closure_with_mutation_and_graph_break(self):\n        def fn():\n            x = torch.zeros(1)\n\n            def subfunc():\n                x[0] = backup\n\n            if x[0] >= -1e5:\n                pass\n\n            backup = 1\n            subfunc()\n            return x\n\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n        expected = fn()\n        actual = opt_fn()\n        self.assertTrue(same(expected, actual))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n\n    def test_closure_out_of_scope_cell_with_cond(self):\n        # Test closure with out-of-scope cell variable, used in a cond\n        # where the two branches read different closure variables\n        from functorch.experimental.control_flow import cond\n\n        def g(x):\n            return x\n\n        class ModuleCondDeep(torch.nn.Module):\n            def forward(self, pred, x):\n                return self._indirection(pred, x)\n\n            def _indirection(self, pred, x):\n                return self.indirection(pred, x)\n\n            def indirection(self, pred, x):\n                def true_fn(y):\n                    return y + 2\n\n                def false_fn(y):\n                    return y - 2\n\n                def shallow(x):\n                    return x * 2\n\n                def deep(x):\n                    # y = g(x)\n                    y = x\n                    return cond(\n                        x[0][0] > 0,\n                        true_fn,\n                        false_fn,\n                        [y],\n                    )\n\n                return cond(pred, shallow, deep, [x])\n\n        mod = ModuleCondDeep()\n        opt_mod = torch.compile(mod, backend=\"eager\")\n        inp = torch.randn(3, 3)\n        exp1 = mod(torch.tensor(False), inp)\n        actual1 = opt_mod(torch.tensor(False), inp)\n        exp2 = mod(torch.tensor(True), inp)\n        actual2 = opt_mod(torch.tensor(True), inp)\n        self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(exp1, actual1))\n        self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(exp2, actual2))\n\n    def test_closure_write_across_functions(self):\n        z = 1\n        k = 2\n\n        def create_fn():\n            def fn(x):\n                nonlocal k, z\n                k = z\n\n            return fn\n\n        def update_z_and_run_fn(fn, x):\n            nonlocal z\n            z = 3\n            fn(x)\n            return x.cos()\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def foo(x):\n            fn = create_fn()\n            return update_z_and_run_fn(fn, x)\n\n        x = torch.randn(1)\n        foo(x)\n        self.assertEqual(3, z)\n        self.assertEqual(3, k)\n\n    def test_free_var_and_local_name_collision(self):\n        x = 10\n\n        def make_func():\n            def func():\n                return x\n\n            return func\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def root(t):\n            x = 0\n            func = make_func()\n            res = func()\n            return t + 1, x, res\n\n        res = root(torch.ones(1))\n        self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(torch.ones(1) + 1, res[0]))\n        self.assertEqual(0, res[1])\n        self.assertEqual(10, res[2])\n\n    def test_cell_captured_by_existing_func_but_not_root_frame(self):\n        x = torch.ones(1)\n\n        def get_inner():\n            def inner():\n                return x + x\n\n            # Calling `inner` so Dynamo won't skip this frame.\n            return inner(), inner\n\n        @torch.compile\n        def root():\n            return get_inner()\n\n        res, inner = root()\n        self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(x + x, res))\n        self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(inner(), res))\n\n    def test_writes_to_cells_across_frames1(self):\n        # This regression test was added when Dynamo accidentally had both\n        # unboxed and normal modeling for pre-existing cells, and failed to\n        # account for buffered writes when we read from the unboxed value.\n        x = 0\n\n        def inc_x():\n            nonlocal x\n            x += 1\n\n        class MyObj:\n            def inc_x_then_return_x(self, fn):\n                fn()\n                return x\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def root(t):\n            obj = MyObj()\n            res = obj.inc_x_then_return_x(inc_x)\n            return t + 1, res\n\n        res = root(torch.zeros(1))\n        self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(res[0], torch.ones(1)))\n        self.assertEqual(res[1], 1)\n        self.assertEqual(x, 1)\n\n    def test_writes_to_cells_across_frames2(self):\n        # This regression test was added when Dynamo didn't fully account for\n        # already established `CellVariable` instance for pre-existing cell,\n        # while encountering the same cell again (we should reuse the instance\n        # rather than creating a new one). This caused buffered writes to escape\n        # the newly created `CellVariable`.\n        x = 0\n\n        def inc_x_and_get_x(obj):\n            nonlocal x\n            x += 1\n            return obj.get_x()\n\n        class MyObj:\n            def get_x(self):\n                return x\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def root(t):\n            obj = MyObj()\n            res = inc_x_and_get_x(obj)\n            return t + 1, res\n\n        res = root(torch.zeros(1))\n        self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(res[0], torch.ones(1)))\n        self.assertEqual(res[1], 1)\n        self.assertEqual(x, 1)\n\n    def test_write_to_cells_with_name_shadowing(self):\n        x = 0\n        y = x\n\n        def make_x_get_set():\n            # NOTE: this `x` is a different cell object than the outer `x`.\n            x = y\n\n            def set_x(v):\n                nonlocal x\n                x = v\n\n            def get_x():\n                return x\n\n            return get_x, set_x\n\n        get_x, set_x = make_x_get_set()\n\n        @torch.compile(fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(t):\n            set_x(42)  # This sets the `x` created within `make_x_get_set`\n            res = t + x  # This uses the `x` outside `make_x_get_set`.\n            return res\n\n        result = fn(torch.ones(1))\n        inner_x = get_x()\n        self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(result, torch.ones(1)))\n        self.assertEqual(inner_x, 42)\n\n    def test_existing_func_that_creates_capturing_nested_func(self):\n        x = 0  # Captured by both   def root(t):\n            get_x = make_get_x()\n            res = t + x\n            return res, get_x\n\n        res, get_x = root(torch.ones(1))\n        self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(res, torch.ones(1)))\n        self.assertEqual(0, get_x())\n        x += 1\n        self.assertEqual(1, get_x())\n\n    def test_input_cell_mutation(self):\n        def fn(x):\n            x = x.cos()\n\n @torch.compile(backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(x):\n            return torch.relu(torch.cos(x) + torch.sin(x))\n\n        fn(torch.randn(4))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n\n        fn(torch.randn(4, 4))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n\n        # Test that run works on a decorated fn\n        fn = torch._dynamo.run(fn)\n        fn(torch.randn(4, 4, torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n\n        def fn(x):\n            return torch.relu(torch.cos(x) + torch.sin(x))\n\n        fn1 = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts1, fullgraph=True)\n        fn2 = torch.compile(fn1, backend=cnts2, fullgraph=True)\n\n        # The first optimize in the nesting should be ignored\n        fn2(torch.randn(4))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts2.frame_count, 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts1.frame_count, 0)\n\n        # Since the fn code object is already compiled, calling fn1 should\n        # directly call the compiled_fn callable.\n        torch._dynamo.run()(fn1)(torch.randn(4))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts1.frame_count, 0)\n\n        # Test same behavior by reversing the calls\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n        cnts1 = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        cnts2 = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        fn1 = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts1, fullgraph=True)\n        fn2 = torch.compile(fn1, backend=cnts2, fullgraph=True)\n        fn1(torch.randn(4))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts1.frame_count, 1)\n        torch._dynamo.run()(fn2)(torch.randn(4))\n        self.assertEqual(cnts2.frame_count, 0)\n\n    def test_torch_size(self):\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n\n        def fn(x):\n            output_size = torch.Size([10, 10])\n
