def test_nested_function_resuming_with_correct_globals(self):\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/99665\n        try:\n            from .utils import outer_func\n        except ImportError:\n            from utils import outer_func\n\n        def gn(x, y):\n            return x + y\n\n        def fn(x, y):\n            return outer_func(gn)(x, y)\n\n        x = torch.rand([3])\n        y = torch.rand([3])\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")(fn)\n        ref = fn(x, y)\n        res = opt_fn(x, y)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n    def test_recursion_depth_guards(self):\n        @torch.compile(dynamic=True)\n        def foo(*args, **kwargs):\n            if sum(args) == 0:\n                return 0\n            return 1\n\n        args = list(range(2000))\n        foo(*args)\n        # Previously would have crashed\n\n    @dataclasses.dataclass\n    class CSETestCase:\n        expr: str\n        preface: typing.List[str] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\n        expected: typing.Optional[str] = None\n\n    def test_guards_cse_pass_single(self):\n        from torch._dynamo.guards import PyExprCSEPass\n\n        testcase = self.CSETestCase\n        testcases = [\n            # Nothing gets CSE-d, since the only repeated sub-expression is 'x'.\n            # i.e. not a node type we are interested on.\n            testcase(expr=\"x[0].a\"),\n            testcase(expr=\"x[1].a\"),\n            testcase(expr=\"x[2].a\"),\n            # 'a.b.c' gets CSE-d, since it's a sub-expression used more than 'PyExprCSEPass.USE_THRESHOLD'.\n            testcase(\n                expr=\"a.b.c[0].d.e\",\n                preface=[\"_var0 = a.b\", \"_var1 = _var0.c\"],\n                expected=\"_var1[0].d.e\",\n            ),\n            testcase(expr=\"a.b.c[1].d.e\", expected=\"_var1[1].d.e\"),\n            testcase(expr=\"a.b.c[2].d.e\", expected=\"_var1[2].d.e\"),\n            # 'm.n[0]' gets CSE-d, since it is a sub-expression used more than 'PyExprCSEPass.USE_THRESHOLD'.\n            testcase(\n                expr=\"f(m.n[0], '0').x.y.z\",\n                preface=[\"_var2 = m.n\", \"_var3 = _var2[0]\"],\n                expected=\"f(_var3, '0').x.y.z\",\n            ),\n            testcase(expr=\"f(m.n[0], '1').x.y.z\", expected=\"f(_var3, '1').x.y.z\"),\n            testcase(expr=\"f(m.n[0], '2').x.y.z\", expected=\"f(_var3, '2').x.y.z\"),\n            # The whole expression gets CSE-d, as well as all of its sub-expressions.\n            testcase(\n                expr=\"self.g(a, b).k\",\n                preface=[\"_var4 = self.g\", \"_var5 = _var4(a, b)\", \"_var6 = _var5.k\"],\n                expected=\"_var6\",\n            ),\n            testcase(expr=\"self.g(a, b).k\", expected=\"_var6\"),\n            testcase(expr=\"self.g(a, b).k\", expected=\"_var6\"),\n        ]\n        csepass = PyExprCSEPass()\n        csepass.count([t.expr for t in testcases])\n\n        for t in testcases:\n            preface, expr = csepass.replace(t.expr)\n            self.assertEqual(preface, t.preface)\n            expected = t.expected if t.expected is not None else t.expr\n            self.assertEqual(expr, expected)\n\n    def test_guards_cse_pass_multiple(self):\n        from torch._dynamo.guards import PyExprCSEPass\n\n        testcase = self.CSETestCase\n        testcases = [\n            testcase(\n                expr=\"x[0].a < x[1].a * (3 - x[2].a)\",\n                expected=\"x[0].a < x[1].a * (3 - x[2].a)\",\n            ),\n            testcase(\n                expr=\"a.b.c[0].d.e + a.b.c[1].d.e * a.b.c[2].d.e > 0\",\n                preface=[\"_var0 = a.b\", \"_var1 = _var0.c\"],\n                expected=\"_var1[0].d.e + _var1[1].d.e * _var1[2].d.e > 0\",\n            ),\n            testcase(\n                expr=\"f(m.n[0], '0').x.y.z * f(m.n[0], '1').x.y.z * f(m.n[0], '2').x.y.z < 512\",\n                preface=[\"_var2 = m.n\", \"_var3 = _var2[0]\"],\n                expected=\"f(_var3, '0').x.y.z * f(_var3, '1').x.y.z * f(_var3, '2').x.y.z < 512\",\n            ),\n            testcase(\n                expr=\"self.g(a, b).k + (1 - self.g(a, b).k) <= m[0].a + self.g(a, b).k\",\n                preface=[\"_var4 = self.g\", \"_var5 = _var4(a, b)\", \"_var6 = _var5.k\"],\n                expected=\"_var6 + (1 - _var6) <= m[0].a + _var6\",\n            ),\n        ]\n\n        csepass = PyExprCSEPass()\n        csepass.count([t.expr for t in testcases])\n\n        for t in testcases:\n            preface, expr = csepass.replace(t.expr)\n            self.assertEqual(preface, t.preface)\n            expected = t.expected\n            expected = expected if expected is not None else t.expr\n            self.assertEqual(expr, expected)\n\n    def test_guard_function_builder_with_cse(self):\n        from torch._dynamo.guards import build_guard_function\n\n        exprs = [\n            \"x[0].a < x[1].a * (3 - x[2].a)\",\n            \"a.b.c[0].d.e + a.b.c[1].d.e * a.b.c[2].d.e > 0\",\n            \"f(m.n[0], '0').x.y.z * f(m.n[0], '1').x.y.z * f(m.n[0], '2').x.y.z < 512\",\n            \"self.g(a, b).k + (1 - self.g(a, b).k) <= m[0].a + self.g(a, b).k\",\n        ]\n\n        _, pycode = build_guard_function(exprs, \"\")\n        expected = \"\"\"\\\ndef ___make_guard_fn():\n    def guard(L):\n        if not (x[0].a < x[1].a * (3 - x[2].a)):\n            return False\n        _var0 = a.b\n        _var1 = _var0.c\n        if not (_var1[0].d.e + _var1[1].d.e * _var1[2].d.e > 0):\n            return False\n        _var2 = m.n\n        _var3 = _var2[0]\n        if not (f(_var3, '0').x.y.z * f(_var3, '1').x.y.z * f(_var3, '2').x.y.z < 512):\n            return False\n        _var4 = self.g\n        _var5 = _var4(a, b)\n        _var6 = _var5.k\n        if not (_var6 + (1 - _var6) <= m[0].a + _var6):\n            return False\n        return True\n    return guard\n\"\"\"\n\n        self.assertEqual(expected, pycode)\n\n    def test_dynamo_compiling_fake_tensor_to_vararg_int(self):\n        class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super().__init__()\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                # use numpy int so it's wrapped as fake tensor in dynamo\n                shape = np.int_(16)\n                # test shape as fake tensor, which param type is\n                # Sequence[Union[_int, SymInt]]\n                return x.reshape(shape)\n\n        x = torch.rand([4, 4])\n        model = MyModule()\n        orig_out = model(x)\n        opt_model = torch.compile(MyModule(), backend=\"eager\")\n        opt_out = opt_model(x)\n        self.assertTrue(same(orig_out, opt_out))\n\n    def test_scalar_tensor_is_equivalent_to_symint_argument(self):\n        class GumbelTopKSampler(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self, T, k):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.T = torch.nn.Parameter(\n                    torch.tensor(T, dtype=torch.float32), requires_grad=False\n                )\n                self.k = torch.nn.Parameter(\n                    torch.tensor(k, dtype=torch.int32), requires_grad=False\n                )\n\n            def sample_discrete(self, logits):\n                threshold = torch.topk(logits, self.k, sorted=True)[0][..., -1]\n                samples = torch.ge(logits.squeeze(1), threshold).float()\n                return samples\n\n            def forward(self, logits):\n                dsamples = self.sample_discrete(logits)\n                return dsamples\n\n        x = torch.rand([4, 4, 4, 4])\n        m = GumbelTopKSampler(T=4, k=4)\n        orig_out = m(x)\n        opt_m = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")(m)\n        opt_out = opt_m(x)\n        self.assertTrue(same(orig_out, opt_out))\n\n    def test_scalar_tensor_is_equivalent_to_symint_list_argument(self):\n        class Jitter(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self, jitter_val):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.jitter_val = jitter_val\n\n            def roll_tensor(self, input):\n                h_shift = self.jitter_val - 1\n                w_shift = self.jitter_val + 1\n                return torch.roll(\n                    torch.roll(input, shifts=h_shift, dims=2), shifts=w_shift, dims=3\n                )\n\n            def forward(self, input):\n                return self.roll_tensor(input)\n\n        x = torch.rand([4, 4, 4, 4])\n        m = Jitter(jitter_val=4)\n        orig_out = m(x)\n        opt_m = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")(m)\n        opt_out = opt_m(x)\n        self.assertTrue(same(orig_out, opt_out))\n\n    def test_scalar_tensor_is_equivalent_to_int_list_argument(self):\n        class MyModel(torch.nn.Module):\n            def forward(self, input):\n                permute = torch.tensor([0, 2, 1])\n                x = input.permute(*permute)\n                return x\n\n        x = torch.randn(2, 3, 4)\n        m = MyModel()\n        orig_out = m(x)\n        opt_m = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")(m)\n        opt_out = opt_m(x)\n        self.assertTrue(same(orig_out, opt_out))\n\n    def            if all([f]) or all([t, f]) or all([f, t]) or all([f, f]):\n                return x - 2\n            if not (all([]) and all([t]) and all([t, t])):\n                return x - 3\n     f2(tensors, dim, num_chunks, out)\n\n    @torch._dynamo.config.patch(capture_scalar_outputs=True)\n    def test_runtime_assert_replacement(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n         setty.add(x[2])\n            setty.add(y)\n            return y * len(setty)\n\n        x = torch.randn(10, 10)\n        y = torch.randn(2, 2)\n        eager_result = foo([x, x, x, x, y], def test_iter_set(self):\n        def foo(x, y):\n            setty = set()\n            for t in x:\n                setty.add(t)\n            return y * len(setty)\n\n        x = torch.randn(10, 10)\n        y = torch.randn(2, 2)\n        eager_result = foo([x, x, x, x, y], y)\n\n        counter = CompileCounter()\n        foo = torch.compile(foo, backend=counter, fullgraph=True)\n        result = foo([x, x, x, x, y], y)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 1)\n        self.assertEqual(result, eager_result)\n\n    def test_reconstruct_set_across_graph_break(self):\n        def foo(x, y):\n            setty = set()\n            for t in x:\n                setty.add(t)\n            print(\"Break!\")\n            return y * len(setty)\n\n        x = torch.randn(10, 10)\n        y = torch.randn(2, 2)\n\n        counter = CompileCounter()\n        foo = torch.compile(foo, backend=counter)\n        result = foo([x, x, x, x, y], y)\n\n    def test_set_aliasing_recompiles(self):\n        g1 = torch.randn(10)\n        g2 = torch.randn(10)\n        g3 = torch.randn(10)\n        g4 = torch.randn(10)\n\n        def foo(a, b, c):\n            myset = {g1, a, b, c}\n            return a + len(myset)\n\n        counter = CompileCounter()\n        foo = torch.compile(foo, backend=counter)\n        # first call with no aliasing\n        foo(g2, g3, g4)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 1)\n\n        # no aliasing again\n        foo(g3, g2, g4)\n        # assert no recompile\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 1)\n\n        # aliasing changes, we should recompile\n        foo(g2, g2, g2)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 2)\n\n        # same aliasing, different tensor\n        foo(g3, g3, g3)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 2)\n\n        # aliasing between global and arg, should recompile again\n        foo(g1, g1, g1)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 3)\n\n        # Reset\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n        # aliasing between global and arg, first call\n        foo(g1, g1, g1)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 4)\n\n        # same aliasing, different tensor, all local, recompile\n        foo(g3, g3, g3)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 5)\n\n        # aliasing same tensor, we shouldn't recompile\n        foo(g2, g2, g2)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 5)\n\n        # No aliasing\n        foo(g2, g3, g4)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 6)\n\n        # No aliasing again\n        foo(g3, g2, g4)\n        # assert no recompile\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 6)\n\n    def test_str_format_return1(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(img):\n            x = torch.sin(img)\n            y = f\"shape {img.shape[-2:]} batch size {img.shape[0]}\"\n            return img + x, y\n\n        img1 = torch.randn(1, 1, 8, 8)\n        res, msg = fn(img1)\n        self.assertEqual(msg, \"shape torch.Size([8, 8]) batch size 1\")\n        self.assertEqual(res, img1 + torch.sin(img1))\n\n    def test_str_format_return2(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(img):\n            x = torch.sin(img)\n            y = \"shape {} batch size {y:.2f}\".format(img.shape[-2:], y=img.shape[0])\n img1 + torch.sin(img1))\n\n    def test_sourceless_namedtuple(self):\n        from collections import namedtuple\n\n        CustomDtype = namedtuple(\"CustomDtype\", [\"dtype\", \"higher_dtype\"])\n\n        class CustomTensor(torch.Tensor):\n            _data: torch.Tensor\n            custom_dtype: CustomDtype\n            __torch_function__ = torch._C._disabled_torch_function_impl\n            __slots__ = [\n                \"_data\",\n                \"custom_dtype\",\n            ]\n\n            def __new__(\n                cls,\n                data: torch.Tensor,\n                custom_dtype: CustomDtype,\n            ):\n                self = torch.Tensor._make_wrapper_subclass(\n                    cls,\n                    data.size(),\n                    strides=data.stride(),\n                    storage_offset=data.storage_offset(),\n                    dtype=custom_dtype.dtype,\n                    layout=data.layout,\n                    requires_grad=data.requires_grad,\n                    device=data.device,\n                )\n                self._data = data\n                self.custom_dtype = custom_dtype\n                return self\n\n            def __tensor_flatten__(self):\n                meta = {\n                    \"custom_dtype\": self.custom_dtype,\n                }\n                return [\"_data\"], meta\n\n            @staticmethod\n            def __tensor_unflatten__(\n                inner_tensors: dict, metadata, outer_size, outer_stride\n            ):\n                return CustomTensor(\n                    inner_tensors[\"_data\"],\n                    metadata[\"custom_dtype\"],\n                )\n\n            @classmethod\n            def __torch_dispatch__(cls, func, types, args=(), kwargs={}):\n                return func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(x):\n            y = CustomTensor(x, CustomDtype(torch.float32, torch.bfloat16))\n            return y, y.custom_dtype\n\n        fn(torch.ones(2, 2, device=\"cpu\"))\n\n    # Compiling autograd.Function traces fwd function twice, but the same unbacked symints were not identified\n    # as the same across the two tracings. This is an unlikely situation in real use cases, so we add another\n    # `test_validate_outputs_unbacked_by_custom_op` to mitigate it and keep this one as expected failure\n    # until we have a proper fix.\n    @unittest.expectedFailure\n    @torch._dynamo.config.patch(capture_scalar_outputs=True)\n    def test_validate_outputs_unbacked(self):\n        class SillyCat(torch.autograd.Function):\n            @staticmethod\n            def forward(ctx, x0, x1, i):\n                ctx.save_for_backward(i)\n                return torch.cat([x0, x1])\n\n            @staticmethod\n            def backward(ctx, grad_out):\n                (i,) = ctx.saved_tensors\n                i0, i1 = i.tolist()\n                g_x0, g_x1 = grad_out.split([i0, i1])\n                return g_x0, g_x1, None\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"aot_eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def f(x, i):\n            i0, i1 = i.tolist()\n            x0, x1 = x.split([i0, i1])\n            return SillyCat.apply(x0, x1, i)\n\n        f(torch.randn(9, requires_grad=True), torch.tensor([3, 6]))\n\n    @torch._dynamo.config.patch(capture_scalar_outputs=True)\n    def test_validate_outputs_unbacked_by_custom_op(self):\n        with torch.library._scoped_library(\"mylib\", \"FRAGMENT\") as lib:\n            torch.library.define(\n                \"mylib::foo\",\n                \"(Tensor a, Tensor b) -> (Tensor)\",\n                tags=torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag,\n                lib=lib,\n            )\n\n            @torch.library.impl(\"mylib::foo\", \"cpu\", lib=lib)\n            @torch.library.register_fake(\"mylib::foo\")\n            def foo_impl(x, y):\n                return torch.cat([x, y])\n\n            @torch.compile(backend=\"aot_eager\", fullgraph=True)\n            def f(x, i):\n                i0, i1 = i.tolist()\n                x0, x1 = x.split([i0, i1])\n                return torch.ops.mylib.foo(x0, x1)\n\n            f(torch.randn(9, requires_grad=True), torch.tensor([3, 6]))\n\n    def test_str_format_assert1(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\",        def deep2(c):\n                return outer(c)\n\n            def deep(c):\n                return deep2(c)\n\n            return deep(x)\n\n        x = torch.randn(3)\n        eager =            return x + 2\n\n            return inner\n\n        @torch.compile\n        def start():\n            # Obtain the `inner` function, which holds reference to `x`.\n            inner = fn()\n\n            # When we call `inner`, we end up looking up `x` from our inlining\n            # tracer, Dynamo must make sure it still has some modeling of `x` at\n            # that point.\n            res = inner()\n            return res\n\n        res = start()\n        self.assertEqual(torch.ones(1) * 3, res)\n\n    def test_deque_input(self):\n        a = torch.randn([2, 3])\n        b = torch.randn([2, 3])\n        d1 = collections.deque([\"foo\", a, b])\n        d2 = d1.copy()\n\n        def fn(q):\n            a = q.pop()\n            b = q.pop()\n            return a * b\n\n        eager = fn(d1)\n        counter = CompileCounter()\n        compiled = torch.compile(fn, backend=counter, fullgraph=True)(d2)\n        self.assertEqual(d1, d2)\n        self.assertEqual(eager, compiled)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 1)\n\n    def test_deque_append_left(self):\n        d1 = collections.deque([\"foo\", 10, 10])\n        d2 = d1.copy()\n\n        def fn(q, a,       def yield_from_gen(l):\n                l2 = [t * k for t in l]\n                yield from l2\n\n            return [t * k for t in yield_from_gen(t_list)]\n\n        t_list = [torch.randn([2, 3]) for _ in range(3)]\n        eager = yield_from_fn(t_list, 2)\n        counter = CompileCounter()\n        compiled = torch.compile(yield_from_fn, backend=counter)(t_list, 2)\n        self.assertEqual(eager, compiled)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 1)\n\n    def test_yield_from_in_a_loop(self):\n        def gen2():\n            yield 1\n\n        def gen1():\n            for value in range(5):\n                yield from gen2()\n\n        def fn(x):\n            c = 0\n            for i in gen1():\n                c = c + i\n            return x + c\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        x = torch.zeros(4)\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n\n    def test_yield_gen_and_from(self):\n        def populate_and_multiply_sequence(n, multiplier):\n            # Inline generator\n            def tensor_generator():\n                for i in range(n):\n                    yield torch.tensor([i])\n\n            # Use 'yield from' to iterate over tensors and multiply\n            t_list = [tensor * multiplier for tensor in tensor_generator()]\n\n            def yield_from_gen():\n                yield from t_list\n\n            return [t for t in yield_from_gen()]\n\n        multiplier = torch.tensor([10])\n        eager = populate_and_multiply_sequence(5, multiplier)\n        counter = CompileCounter()\n        compiled = torch.compile(populate_and_multiply_sequence, backend=counter)(\n            5, multiplier\n        )\n        self.assertEqual(eager, compiled)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 1)\n\n    def test_yield_from_user_stop_iteration(self):\n        class MyIter:\n            def __init__(self, seq):\n                self.seq = seq\n                self.index = 0\n\n            def __iter__(self):\n                return self\n\n            def __next__(self):\n                self.index += 1\n                if self.index <= len(self.seq):\n                    return self.seq[self.index - 1]\n                raise StopIteration(self.index)\n\n        def yield_from_iter_fn(seq):\n            def gen(seq):\n                yield from MyIter(seq)\n\n            return [i for i in gen(seq)]\n\n        seq = [torch.randn([2, 3]) for _ in range(3)]\n        eager = yield_from_iter_fn(seq)\n        counter = CompileCounter()\n        compiled = torch.compile(yield_from_iter_fn, backend=counter)(seq)\n        self.assertEqual(eager, compiled)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 0)\n\n    # just to be sure in case anyone tries to run this in older versions of Python\n    def test_pep0479_convert_stopiteration(self):\n        # https://peps.python.org/pep-0479/\n        def generator_with_stop_iteration():\n            yield 1\n            # Explicitly raising StopIteration inside the generator\n            raise StopIteration(\"StopIteration raised within generator\")\n            yield 2  # This should never be reached\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(t):\n            try:\n                # Try to consume the generator\n                gen = generator_with_stop_iteration()\n                next(gen)\n                next(gen)\n            except RuntimeError as e:\n                # Check that StopIteration was converted to RuntimeError\n                # See STOPITERATION_ERROR opcode in symbolic_convert.py\n                return 100\n            except StopIteration:\n                return 200\n\n        t = torch.randn(2)\n        y = fn(t)\n        self.assertEqual(y, 100)\n\n    def test_yield_send_to_subgenerator_graph_break(self):\n        def subgenerator(tensor):\n            multiplier = yield\n            yield tensor * multiplier\n\n        def main_generator(t_list):\n            for tensor in t_list:\n                subgen = subgenerator(tensor)\n                next(subgen)\n                yield from subgen.send(torch.tensor([10]))\n\n        t_list = [torch.tensor([i]) for i in range(5)]\n        eager = list(main_generator(t_list))\n\n        counter = CompileCounter()\n        compiled_fn = torch.compile(main_generator, backend=counter)\n        compiled = list(compiled_fn(t_list))\n\n        self.assertEqual(eager, compiled)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 0)\n\n    def test_derpy_nn_module_usage(self):\n        def ff1(x):\n            self = mod1\n            return torch.sigmoid(self.mod2(x) + self.param1)\n\n        def ff2(x):\n            self = mod2\n            return torch.cos(torch.sin(x) * self.param2 + 10)\n\n        mod1 = torch.nn.Module()\n        mod2 = torch.nn.Module()\n        mod1.register_module(\"mod2\", mod2)\n        mod1.register_parameter(\"param1\", torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(10)))\n        mod1.forward = ff1\n        mod2.register_parameter(\"param2\", torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(10)))\n        mod2.forward = ff2\n        mod1.eval()\n\n        x = torch.randn(10)\n        expected = mod1(x)\n        counter = CompileCounter()\n        actual = torch.compile(mod1, backend=counter, fullgraph=True)(x)\n        self.assertEqual(actual, expected)\n        self.assertEqual(counter.op_count, 6)\n\n    def test_default_args_device_dtype(self):\n iter([]),\n                iter([10, 11.0]),\n                itertools.repeat(-1, 3),\n                itertools.count(10),\n            ):\n                r = itertools.cycle(iterator)\n                idx = 0\n                x += 1\n                for i in r:\n                    x += i\n                    idx += 1\n                    if idx > 10:\n                        break\n            return x\n\n        x = torch.randn([2, 5])\n        eager = fn(x)\n\n        compiled_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        compiled = compiled_fn(x)\n\n x *= i\n            return x\n\n        x = torch.randn(2, 3)\n        eager = fn(x)\n\n        compiled_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        compiled =      counters.clear()\n\n        def fn(a, b, c, d, x):\n            l = [a, b, c, d, x]\n            for i, t in enumerate(l):\n                l[i] = t * x\n            return itertools.accumulate(l)\n\n        t_list = [torch.tensor([i + 1]) for i in range(4)]\n        x = torch.tensor([[1, 2], [3, 4]])\n        eager = fn(*t_list, x)\n\n        compiled_fn = self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 0)\n\n    def test_itertools_accumulate_tensors_builtins(self):\n        for builtin_op in [operator.mul, operator.sub, operator.pow]:\n            counters.clear()\n\n            def fn(a, b, c, d, x):\n                l = [a, b, c, d, x]\n                for i, t in enumerate(l):\n                    l[i] = t * x\n                return compiled_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n            compiled = compiled_fn(*t_list, x)\n\n            self.assertEqual(list(eager), list(compiled))\n            self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 0)\n\n    def test_itertools_accumulate_tensors_kwargs(self):\n        from torch._dynamo.utils import counters\n\n        for kwargs in [\n            l = [a, b, c, d, x]\n                for i, t in enumerate(l):\n                    l[i] = t * x\n                return itertools.accumulate(l, **kwargs)\n\n            t_list = test_itertools_accumulate_tensors_user_defined(self):\n        def udo_fn_0(a, b):\n            return -1\n\n        rando = random.randint(0, 1)\n\n        def udo_fn_1(a, b):\n            return a * rando + b * rando\n\n        seen = []\n\n        def udo_fn_2(a, b):\n            seen.append(a)\n            seen.append(b)\n            return a * len(seen)\n\n        for udo_fn in [udo_fn_0, udo_fn_1, udo_fn_2]:\n            counters.clear()\n            torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n            def fn(a, b, c, d, x):\n                l = [a, b, c, d, x]\n                for i, t in enumerate(l):\n                    l[i] = t * x\n                return itertools.accumulate(l, udo_fn)\n\n            t_list = [torch.tensor([i]) for i in range(4)]\n            x = torch.tensor([[1, 2], [3, 4]])\n            eager = fn(*t_list, x)\n\n            compiled_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n            compiled = compiled_fn(*t_list, x)\n\n            self.assertEqual(list(eager), list(compiled))\n            self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 0)\n\n    def test_pure_python_accumulate(self):\n        def accumulate(iterable, func=lambda x, y: x + y):\n            it = iter(iterable)\n            try:\n                # Initialize the accumulator with the first value from the iterable\n                accumulator = next(it)\n            except StopIteration:\n                # If the iterable is empty, return an empty generator\n                return\n            yield accumulator\n\n            for element in it:\n                accumulator = func(accumulator, element)\n                yield accumulator\n\n        def fn(it):\n            return accumulate(it)\n\n        t_list = [torch.tensor([i]) for i in range(4)]\n        eager = fn(t_list)\n\n        counter = CompileCounter()\n        compiled_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=counter)\n        compiled = compiled_fn(t_list)\n\n        self.assertEqual(list(eager), list(compiled))\n        self.assertEqual(counter.frame_count, 1)\n\n    def test_itertools_groupby_pure_python_default_identify_func(self):\n        counters.clear()\n\n        def fn(l):\n            return [(k, list(g)) for k, g in itertools.groupby(l)]\n\n        l = [1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 4, 4, 1, 2]\n        eager = fn(l)\n\n        compiled_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        compiled = compiled_fn(l)\n\n        self.assertEqual(eager, compiled)\n        self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 0)\n\n    def torch.zeros(3)\n        compiled_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x), compiled_fn(x))\n\n    def test_storage_return(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(x):\n            y = torch.sin(x + 1)\n            storage = x.untyped_storage()\n            storage.resize_(0)\n            y = torch.cos(y)\n            return y, storage\n\n        x = torch.randn(10)\n        expected = torch.cos(torch.sin(x + 1))\n        y, s = fn(x)\n        self.assertEqual(y, expected)\n   def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super().__init__()\n                self.register_parameter(\"0\", torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(3, 4)))\n                self.test_buf = torch.nn.Buffer(torch.randn(3, 4))\n                self.register_parameter(\n                    \"test_param\", torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(3, 4))\n                )\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                return ((x + self.test_buf) * getattr(self, \"0\")) / self.test_param\n\n        class TestModule(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super().__init__()\n                self.foo_bar = FooBarModule()\n                self.register_parameter(\n                    \"test_param\", torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(3, 4))\n                )\n                self.test_buf = torch.nn.Buffer(torch.randn(3, 4))\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                return (self.foo_bar(x) + self.test_param) * self.test_buf\n\n        gm, _ = torch._dynamo.export(TestModule(), torch.randn(3, 4))\n        self.assertIn(\"dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn\", gm.meta)\n        expected_fqn = {\n            \"L__self___test_param\":        torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n            x.sin()\n            torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n            return x.cos()\n\n        torch._dynamo.utils.clear_compilation_metrics()\n        x = torch.rand((4, 4))\n        f = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        f(x)\n        metrics = torch._dynamo.utils.get_compilation_metrics()\n        # Should only be one restart per event\n        (restart_reason,) = metrics[0].restart_reasons\n        self.assertTrue(\n            \"User-inserted graph break\" in restart_reason,\n            \"Should have logged graph break reason\",\n metrics_limit_ctx():\n            try:\n                torch._dynamo.utils.set_compilation_metrics_limit(3)\n                yield\n            finally:\n                torch._dynamo.utils.set_compilation_metrics_limit(\n                    torch._dynamo.utils.DEFAULT_COMPILATION_METRICS_LIMIT\n                )\n\n        x = torch.rand((4, 4))\n    y = 0\n            if guard_size_oblivious(torch.sym_max(1, u0 + u1) == 20):\n                y += 1\n            if guard_size_oblivious(torch.sym_max(1, u0**2 + u1 + 2) != 1):\n                y += 1\n            if guard_size_oblivious(torch.sym_min(1, u0) == 1):\n                y += 1\n            return y\n\n        # Previously would have thrown guard on data dependent\n constructor that returns a new model and inputs to that model\n        weakref_watch\n            - function that returns a layer of the model for weakref to\n              finalize on, so we can check that the layer is freed after\n              the model goes out of scope\n        \"\"\"\n        cleared = False\n\n        def finalize():\n            nonlocal cleared\n            cleared = True\n\n        def run():\n            mod, inp = model_inp_ctr()\n            weakref.finalize(weakref_watch(mod), finalize)\n            torch.compile(mod, backend=\"eager\")(inp)\n\n        run()\n        gc.collect()\n        self.assertTrue(cleared)\n\n    def test_custom_module_free(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that a model is freed when it goes out of scope\"\"\"\n\n        class Mod(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super(Mod, self).__init__()\n                self.fc = torch.nn.Linear(100, 100)\n\n            def forward(self, out):\n                return self.fc(out)\n\n        self._test_compile_model_free(\n            lambda: (Mod(), torch.randn(100, 100)),\n            lambda mod: mod.fc,\n        )\n\n    def test_sequential_module_free(self):\n        self._test_compile_model_free(\n            lambda: (\n                torch.nn.Sequential(\n                    torch.nn.Linear(100, 100),\n                    torch.nn.ReLU(),\n                ),\n                torch.randn(100, 100),\n            ),\n            lambda mod: mod[0],\n        )\n\n    def test_linear_module_free(self):\n    # Compared to test_linear_module_free, the linear\n        # layer is not the code object that is directly compiled.\n\n        # This test does not use _test_compile_model_free because of difficulty\n        # in handling variable fc.\n\n        cleared = False\n\n        def finalize():\n            nonlocal cleared\n            cleared = True\n\n        def run():\n            fc = torch.nn.Linear(100, 100)\n\n            class Mod(torch.nn.Module):\n                def __init__(self) -> None:\n                    super().__init__()\n                    self.fc_ref = fc\n\n                def forward(self, x):\n                    return self.fc_ref(x)\n\n            mod = Mod()\n            inp = torch.randn(100, 100)\n            weakref.finalize(fc, finalize)\n            torch.compile(mod, backend=\"eager\")(inp)\n\n        run()\n        # del fc  # This should delete all the references\n        gc.collect()\n        self.assertTrue(cleared)\n\n    def test_parameter_free(self):\n        def model_inp_ctr():\n            param = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.randn(100, 100))\n\n            class Mod(torch.nn.Module):\n                def __init__(self) self.subTest(scenario):\n                self._test_cleanup_on_failure(scenario)\n\n    def _test_cleanup_on_failure(self, scenario):\n        node_refs = []\n        tracked_fakes_ref = [None]\n\n        def capturing_backend(gm, example_inputs):\n            for node in gm.graph.nodes:\n                node_refs.append(weakref.ref(node))\n            raise RuntimeError(\"deliberate failure\")\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=capturing_backend)\n        def fn(x):\n            return x.sin()\n\n        try:\n            fn(torch.randn(4))\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n        if scenario == \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\":\n            # After cleanup, nodes should be freed without a full gc pass\n            collected_cycles = gc.collect(0)\n            self.assertEqual(collected_cycles, 0)\n        elif scenario == \"repeated_failure_no_leak\":\n            for _ in range(5):\n                try:\n                    fn(torch.randn(4))\n                except Exception:\n                    pass\n\n    def test_conditional_list_comp_in_context(self):\n        def fn(inp):\n            try:\n                return [torch.sin(x) for x in inp if x test_dynamo_cache_move_to_front(self):\n        def fn(x, const):\n            return x + const\n\n        # dynamic=False forces Dynamo to recompile\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", dynamic=False)\n\n        inp = torch.randn(3, 3)\n\n        # NOTE: assumes that each cache entry is guarded\n        # on unique Mod instance\n        opt_fn(inp, 1)\n        opt_fn(inp, 2)\n        opt_fn(inp, 3)\n\n        c1 = _debug_get_cache_entry_list(fn.__code__)\n        self.assertEqual(len(c1), 3)\n\n        # move cache entry to front\n        opt_fn(inp, 2)\n        c2 = _debug_get_cache_entry_list(fn.__code__)\n        self.assertIs(c1[1], c2[0])\n\n    @torch._dynamo.config.patch(inline_inbuilt_nn_modules=False)\n    @skipIfWindows(msg=\"TODO: (xuhancn) conform, AssertionError: False is not true\")\n    def test_dynamo_cache_invalidate(self):\n        DeletedGuardManagerWrapper = torch._dynamo.guards.DeletedGuardManagerWrapper\n\n        class Mod(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super(Mod, self).__init__()\n                self.fc = torch.nn.Linear(3, 3)\n\n            def forward(self, out):\n                return self.fc(out)\n\n        def fn(x, mod):\n            return mod(x)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n\n        m1 = Mod()\n        m2 = Mod()\n        m3 = Mod()\n        inp = torch.randn(3, 3)\n\n        # NOTE: assumes that each cache entry is guarded\n        # on unique Mod instance\n        opt_fn(inp, m1)\n        opt_fn(inp, m2)\n        opt_fn(inp, m3)\n\n        c1 = _debug_get_cache_entry_list(fn.__code__)\n        self.assertEqual(len(c1), 3)\n\n        # move cache entry to front\n        opt_fn(inp, m2)\n        c2 = _debug_get_cache_entry_list(fn.__code__)\n        self.assertIs(c1[1], c2[0])\n\n        # delete center of cache\n        del m3\n        c3 = _debug_get_cache_entry_list(fn.__code__)\n        self.assertEqual(len(c3), 3)\n        self.assertTrue(isinstance(c3[2].guard_manager, DeletedGuardManagerWrapper))\n\n        # delete end of cache\n        del m1\n        c4 = _debug_get_cache_entry_list(fn.__code__)\n        self.assertEqual(len(c4), 3)\n        self.assertTrue(isinstance(c4[1].guard_manager, DeletedGuardManagerWrapper))\n        self.assertTrue(isinstance(c4[2].guard_manager, DeletedGuardManagerWrapper))\n\n        del m2\n        c5 = _debug_get_cache_entry_list(fn.__code__)\n self.assertTrue(isinstance(c5[2].guard_manager, DeletedGuardManagerWrapper))\n\n    def test_inspect_signature_bind(self):\n        import inspect\n\n        def inner(a, b, *ar, c=10, d=11, **kw):\n     y = torch.ones(2, 2)\n        x = torch.randn(2, 2)\n        x.grad = torch.arange(4).reshape(2, 2).to(torch.float)\n\n        z = fn(x, y)\n        ref_y = torch.clone(z).detach()\n        ref_x_grad = torch.clone(x.grad).detach()\n\n        y = torch.ones(2, 2)\n        x = torch.randn(2, 2)\n        x.grad = torch.arange(4).reshape(2, 2).to(torch.float)\n\n        cnt = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounterWithBackend(\"eager\")\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnt)\n        z = opt_fn(x, y)\n\n        # Ensure that the generated graph returns only one output. We want the\n        # add_ on the grad to be part of the graph itself, so that inductor can\n        # theoretically move the add_ and resulting copy_ nodes at the right\n        # place to free    res = fn(torch.ones(2))\n        self.assertEqual(torch.ones(2) + 2, res)\n\n    def test_descriptor(self):\n        class lazy_property:\n            def __init__(self, wrapped):\n                self.wrapped = wrapped\n\n            def __get__(self, instance, obj_type=None):\n                value = self.wrapped(instance)\n                setattr(instance, self.wrapped.__name__, value)\n                return value\n\n        class UserDefined:\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                self.a = 3\n\n            @lazy_property\n            def length(self):\n                return 3\n\n            def run(self, x):\n                return x * self.length\n\n        obj = UserDefined()\n\n        def fn(x):\n            return obj.run(x)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        x = torch.randn(4)\n        # Opt_fn is deliberately called first to trigger the __get__ function.\n        # Otherwise, the setattr removes the lazy property.\n        ref = opt_fn(x)\n        res = fn(x)\n        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n        ref = opt_fn(x)\n        res = fn(x)\n        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n\n    def test_descriptor_side_effect(self):\n        # This pattern (readonly descriptor but writable value in `__dict__`) is\n        # from scipy `_make_tuple_bunch`:\n        # https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/maintenance/1.9.x/scipy/_lib/_bunch.py#L32-L226\n        def fget(obj):\n            return obj.__dict__[\"field\"]\n\n        class MyClass:\n            def __init__(self, n):\n                self.__dict__[\"field\"] = n\n\n            field = property(fget)\n\n        def fn(x):\n            obj = MyClass(42)\n            return x + obj.field, obj\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        x = torch.randn(4)\n        ref_t, ref_obj = fn(x)\n        res_t, res_obj = opt_fn(x)\n        self.assertEqual(ref_t, res_t)\n        self.assertEqual(ref_obj.field, res_obj.field)\n\n    def test_assert_size_stride(self):\n        x = torch.randn(2, 3, 4)\n        with self.assertRaisesRegex(\n            AssertionError,\n            \"expected size 2==5, stride 12==9 at dim=0; expected size 3==6, stride 4==9 at dim=1; expected size 4==7, stride 1==10 at dim=2\",\n        ):\n            torch._C._dynamo.guards.assert_size_stride(x, (5, 6, 7), (9, 9, 10))\n\n    def test_frozen_dict(self):\n        # A pattern from StableDiffusion\n        class FrozenDict(collections.OrderedDict):\n            def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):\n                super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)\n\n                for key, value in self.items():\n                    setattr(self, key, value)\n\n                self.__frozen = True\n\n            def __delitem__(self, *args, **kwargs):\n                raise Exception(\n                    f\"You cannot use ``__delitem__`` on a {self.__class__.__name__} instance.\"\n                )\n\n            def setdefault(self, *args, **kwargs):\n                raise Exception(\n                    f\"You cannot use ``setdefault`` on a {self.__class__.__name__} instance.\"\n                )\n\n            def pop(self, *args, **kwargs):\n                raise Exception(\n                    f\"You cannot use ``pop`` on a {self.__class__.__name__} instance.\"\n                )\n\n            def update(self, *args, **kwargs):\n                raise Exception(\n                    f\"You cannot use ``update`` on a {self.__class__.__name__} instance.\"\n                )\n\n            def __setattr__(self, name, value):\n                if hasattr(self, \"__frozen\") and self.__frozen:\n                    raise Exception(\n                        f\"You cannot use ``__setattr__`` on a {self.__class__.__name__} instance.\"\n                    )\n                super().__setattr__(name, value)\n\n            def __setitem__(self, name, value):\n                if hasattr(self, \"__frozen\") and self.__frozen:\n                    raise Exception(\n                        f\"You cannot use ``__setattr__`` on a {self.__class__.__name__} instance.\"\n                    )\n                super().__setitem__(name, value)\n\n        d = {\"a\": 1}\n        frozen_d = FrozenDict(d)\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(x):\n            dict(frozen_d).items()\n            return torch.sin(x)\n\n        fn(torch.randn(4))\n\n    def test_tuple_class(self):\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n\n        def fn(x):\n            updated_x = []\n            for v in x:\n                updated_x.append(v + 1)\n            return x.__class__(updated_x)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)\n\n        d1 = torch.zeros(2, 2)\n        d2 = torch.ones(2, 2)\n\n        r = opt_fn((d1, d2))\n        self.assertEqual(r.__class__, tuple)\n        r1, r2 = r\n        self.assertEqual(r1, torch.ones(2, 2))\n        self.assertEqual(r2, torch.ones(2, 2) + 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n\n    def test_list_class(self):\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n\n        def fn(x):\n            updated_x = []\n            for v in = torch.ones(2, 2)\n\n        r = opt_fn([d1, d2])\n        self.assertEqual(r.__class__, list)\n        self.assertEqual(len(r), 2)\n        self.assertEqual(r[0], torch.ones(2, 2))\n        self.assertEqual(r[1], torch.ones(2, 2) + 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n\n    def test_namedtuple_class(self):\n        import collections\n\n        cnts =      self.assertEqual(r.y, torch.ones(2, 2) + 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n\n    def test_getattrvariable_as_python_constant(self):\n        from torch._dynamo.variables.misc import GetAttrVariable\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def fn(x, rand1):\n            random.Random().setstate(rand1.getstate())\n            return x + rand1.random()\n\n        def get_rng():\n            rand1 = random.Random(1)\n            orig_random = rand1.random\n            rand1.random = lambda: orig_random()\n            return rand1\n\n        x = torch.randn(3, 3)\n        expected = fn.__wrapped__(x, get_rng())\n\n        with patch.object(GetAttrVariable, \"as_python_constant\", autospec=True) as po:\n            actual = fn(x, get_rng())\n\n        self.assertEqual(expected, actual)\n        self.assertGreater(po.call_count, 0)\n\n    def test_data_ptr_graph_break_builtin(self):\n        def f(a, b):\n            # builtin + not implemented for DataPtrVariable\n            return a.data_ptr() + b.data_ptr()\n\n        a = torch.randn(4)\n        b = torch.randn(5)\n\n        # make sure there is a graph break\n        with self.assertRaises(torch._dynamo.exc.Unsupported):\n            torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)(a, b)\n\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n        expected = f(a, b)\n        actual = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\")(a, b)\n\n        self.assertEqual(expected, actual)\n\n    def test_data_ptr_graph_break_aten(self):\n        def f(a):\n            # torch.add not implemented for DataPtrVariable\n            return torch.add(a, a.data_ptr())\n\n        a = torch.randn(4)\n\n        counters.clear()\n\n        expected = f(a)\n        actual = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\")(a)\n\n        self.assertEqual(expected, actual)\n        self.assertTrue(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]) > 0)\n        counters.clear()\n\n    class AssertNumOutputBackend:\n        \"\"\"\n        A backend that checks the number of output for compiled graph, and\n        return the graph as is.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        def __init__(self, test_case, expected_num_output: int):\n            self.test_case = test_case\n            self.expected_num_output = expected_num_output\n\n        def __call__(self, gm: torch.fx.GraphModule, example_inputs):\n            outputs = gm(*example_inputs)\n            self.test_case.assertEqual(self.expected_num_output, len(outputs))\n            return gm\n\n    def test_returning_nested_func_with_captured_tensor(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=self.AssertNumOutputBackend(self, 2))\n        def test():\n            x = torch.rand(1)\n\n            def func():\n                return x + x\n\n            # Returning `func` forces dynamo to output `x` in the compiled\n            # graph, so that we can store it as `func`'s closure. The output of\n            # compiled graph would be `(x, x + x)`.\n            return func, func()\n\n        test()\n\n    def test_running_nested_func_with_captured_tensor(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=self.AssertNumOutputBackend(self, 1))\n        def test():\n            x = torch.rand(1)\n\n            def func():\n                return x + x\n\n            # `x` is no longer needed after running the compiled graph, so we\n            # shouldn't return it. The output of compiled graph would be `(x +\n            # x,)`.\n            return func()\n\n        test()\n\n    def test_returning_func_with_captured_func_and_tensor(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=self.AssertNumOutputBackend(self, 2))\n        def test():\n            x = torch.rand(1)\n\n            def nested():\n                return x + x\n\n            def func():\n                return nested()\n\n            # Returning `func` forces dynamo to output `x` in the compiled\n            # graph, so that we can store it as `func`'s closure. The output of\n            # compiled graph would be `(x, x + x)`.\n            return func, func()\n\n        test()\n\n    def test_running_func_with_captured_func_and_tensor(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=self.AssertNumOutputBackend(self, 1))\n        def test():\n            x = torch.rand(1)\n\n            def nested():\n                return x + x\n\n            def func():\n                return nested()\n\n            # `x` is no longer needed after running the compiled graph, so we\n            # shouldn't return it. The output of compiled graph would be `(x)`.\n            return func()\n\n        test()\n\n    def test_escaping_closure_var_with_backward_hook(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=self.AssertNumOutputBackend(self, 2))\n        def fn(x):\n            temp = x * x\n            captured_var = temp + 1\n\n            # This is where the lambda escapes the lifetime of `fn`, so\n            # dynamo must generate proper bytecode to update `captured_var`.\n            x.register_hook(lambda _: captured_var)\n\n            # The output of compiled graph would be `(x * x, x * x + 1)`.\n            return temp\n\n        ones = torch.ones(4, requires_grad=True)\n        fn(ones).sum().backward()\n\n    def test_escaping_closure_var_with_nonlocal_var(self):\n        nonlocal_fn = None\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=self.AssertNumOutputBackend(self, 2))\n        def fn(x):\n            temp = x * x\n            captured_var = x + 1\n\n            def inner():\n                return captured_var\n\n            # This is where `inner` escapes the lifetime of `fn`, so dynamo must\n            # generate proper bytecode to update `captured_var`.\n            nonlocal nonlocal_fn\n            nonlocal_fn = inner\n\n            # The output of compiled graph would be `(x * x, x * x + 1)`.\n            return temp\n\n        ones = torch.ones(4, requires_grad=True)\n        fn(ones)\n        nonlocal_fn()\n\n    def test_compare_tensor_with_none(self):\n        @torch.compile()\n        def f(x):\n            return torch.tensor(x == None)\n\n        res = f(torch.tensor(1))\n        self.assertEqual(torch.tensor(False), res)\n\n    def test_dataclass(self):\n        @dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\n        class Foo:\n            x: int\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def run(x, foo0):\n            if dataclasses.is_dataclass(foo0):\n                foo1 = dataclasses.replace(foo0, **{\"x\": 1})\n                return x + 1, foo1\n            return x + 2, foo0\n\n        res, foo = run(torch.zeros(1), Foo(0))\n        self.assertTrue(res, torch.ones(1))\n        self.assertEqual(foo.x, 1)\n\n    def test_frozenset_of_non_literals(self):\n        class Foo:\n            pass\n\n        foo = Foo()\n        foo.x = 0\n        s = frozenset([foo])\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def run(x, s, foo0):\n            # Dynamo must have the same representation for `foo0` and `foo1`,\n            # otherwise the update to `foo0.x` won't be reflected in the read of\n            # `foo1.x`.\n            foo1 = list(s)[0]\n            foo0.x += 1\n            return x + 1, foo1.x\n\n        res = run(torch.ones(1), s, foo)\n        self.assertTrue(same(res[0], torch.ones(1) + 1))\n        self.assertEqual(res[1], 1)\n\n    def test_ne_operator_with_custom_eq(self):\n        class Foo:\n            def __init__(self, x):\n                self.x = x\n\n            def __eq__(self, other):\n                return self.x == other.x\n\n        @torch.compile(fullgraph=True, backend=\"eager\")\n        def run(x):\n            f1 = Foo(0)\n            f2 = Foo(0)\n            # `x + 1` prevents Dynamo from skipping this frame.\n            return x + 1, f1 != f2\n\n        _, ne = run(torch.ones(1))\n        self.assertFalse(ne)\n\n    def test_ne_operator_with_custom_ne(self):\n        class Foo:\n            def __init__(self, x):\n                self.x = x\n                self.ne_called = False\n\n            def __ne__(self, other):\n                # ne_called attr is later checked to ensure that overridden\n                # `__ne__` is traced\n                self.ne_called = True\n                return not self.__eq__(other)\n\n            def __eq__(self, other):\n                return self.x == other.x\n\n        f1 = Foo(0)\n        f2 = Foo(0)\n\n        @torch.compile(fullgraph=True, backend=\"eager\")\n        def run(x):\n            # `x + 1` prevents Dynamo from skipping this frame.\n            return x + 1, f1 != f2\n\n        _, ne = run(torch.ones(1))\n        self.assertFalse(ne)\n        self.assertTrue(f1.ne_called)\n\n    def test_ne_operator_with_custom_graphbreak_eq(self):\n        counters.clear()\n\n        class Foo:\n            def __init__(self, x):\n                self.x = x\n\n            def __eq__(self, other):\n                # This allows us to check that Dynamo actually traced into the\n                # custom eq method.\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                return self.x == other.x\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def run(x):\n            f1 = Foo(0)\n            f2 = Foo(0)\n            # `x + 1` prevents Dynamo from skipping this frame.\n            return x + 1, f1 != f2\n\n        _, ne = run(torch.ones(1))\n        self.assertFalse(ne)\n        self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 1)\n\n    @unittest.skipIf(sys.version_info < (3, 11), \"Python 3.11+\")\n    def test_RAISE_VARARGS_0(self):\n        def foo():\n            try:\n                raise ValueError\n            except:\n                raise\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(t):\n            try:\n                foo()\n            except ValueError:\n                return t.sin()\n            except          def __init__(self, v):\n                self.v = v\n\n        class Foo:\n            attribute_map = {}\n\n            def __init__(self):\n                self.attribute_map = {\n                    \"a_premap\": \"a\",\n                }\n                # `bar` attribute requires propagating sources correctly through\n                # object.__getattribute__\n                self.bar = Bar(5)\n\n            def __setattr__(self, key, value):\n                if key in super().__getattribute__(\"attribute_map\"):\n                    key = super().__getattribute__(\"attribute_map\")[key]\n                super().__setattr__(key, value)\n\n            def __getattribute__(self, key):\n                if key == \"sentinel\":\n                    raise AttributeError()\n                if key != \"attribute_map\" and key in super().__getattribute__(\n                    \"attribute_map\"\n                ):\n                    key = super().__getattribute__(\"attribute_map\")[key]\n                return super().__getattribute__(key)\n\n            def __getattr__(self, key):\n                if key == \"sentinel\":\n                    return 5\n                raise AttributeError()\n\n        def get_foo():\n            f = Foo()\n            f.a_premap = 2\n            f.b = 3\n            return f\n\n        def fn(x, f):\n            return x * f.a_premap * f.a * f.b * f.sentinel * f.bar.v\n\n        x = torch.randn(4)\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x, get_foo()), opt_fn(x, get_foo()))\n\n    def test_dunder_weakref(self):\n        class Foo:\n            pass\n\n        def fn(x):\n            foo = Foo()\n            # tests    x = torch.randn(4)\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n\n    def test_guard_filter_fn_by_id(self):\n        def guard_filter_fn(entries):\n            return [entry.guard_type != test_jit_save(self):\n        def fn():\n            class Foo(torch.nn.Module):\n                def __init__(self) -> None:\n                    super().__init__()\n                    self.a = 3\n\n                @torch.jit.export\n                def __getstate__(self):\n                    return (3, self.training)\n\n                @torch.jit.export\n                def __setstate__(self, state):\n                    self.a = state[0]\n                    self.training = state[1]\n\n                def forward(self, x):\n                    return x + self.a\n\n            f = Foo()\n\n            return torch.jit.trace(f, (torch.rand(3, 4),))\n\n        fn()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        opt_fn()\n\n\nclass TestCustomFunction(torch.testing._internal.common_utils.TestCase):\n    def test_autograd_function_with_matmul_folding_at_output(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        When tensor folding occurs during matmul operation returned tensor is a view.\n        This can cause issues when matmul is used inside a custom function\n        and such view is then returned as output. Then it cannot be modified inplace\n        and causes errors.\n        It can be especially problematic when after such function inplace allreduce\n        is performed. This test recreates this behaviour.\n        Issue is resolved when unsafe_view is returned from matmul instead.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        class CustomFunction(torch.autograd.Function):\n            @staticmethod\n            def forward(ctx, inp1, inp2):\n                ctx.save_for_backward(inp2)\n                ctx.output_shape = inp1.size()\n                return torch.matmul(inp1, inp2)\n\n            @staticmethod\n            def backward(ctx, grad_output):\n                output_shape = ctx.output_shape\n                (inp2,) = ctx.saved_tensors\n                return (\n                    torch.mm(grad_output.squeeze(), inp2.t()).view(output_shape),\n                    None,\n                )\n\n        def outer_function(inp1, inp2):\n            res = CustomFunction.apply(inp1, inp2)\n            res.add_(1.0)\n            return res.sum()\n\n        def usual_function(inp1, inp2) -> torch.Tensor:\n            res = torch.matmul(inp1, inp2)\n            res.add_(1.0)\n            return res.sum()\n\n        inp1_custom = torch.randn(4, 1, 2, requires_grad=True)\n        inp1_usual = inp1_custom.detach().clone().requires_grad_(True)\n\n        inp2 = torch.randn(2, 4)\n        c_custom_func = torch.compile(outer_function)\n        c_usual_func = torch.compile(usual_function)\n\n        result_custom = c_custom_func(inp1_custom, inp2)\n        result_custom.backward()\n        result_usual = c_usual_func(inp1_usual, inp2)\n        result_usual.backward()\n\n        torch.allclose(inp1_custom.grad, inp1_usual.grad)\n\n    def test_retain_grad(self):\n        def fn(x, y):\n            y.retain_grad()\n            return torch.sin(y) + x\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"aot_eager\")\n        x = torch.randn(4, requires_grad=True)\n        y = torch.cos(x)\n        opt_fn(x, y).sum().backward()\n        self.assertTrue(y.grad is not None)\n\n\nclass ref_run2))\n\n        torch.manual_seed(10)\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)\n        res = opt_fn()\n\n        self.assertTrue(same(res, ref_run1))\n\n But when tracing fails, the exception propagates through that `finally` block before reaching the `except Exception` handler in `convert_frame.py`, which means `cleanup()` runs before we can catch the exception there.\n\nThe key insight is that both success and failure paths trigger `cleanup()` through the `finally` block. So if I add `cleanup_graph_nodes()` inside `cleanup()`, it will be called in both cases without duplication—I just need to make sure I'm not also calling it separately in the exception handler. the graph object is already compiled into the output, so cleanup is harmless either way. if verify_correctness=True\n        try:\n            correct = self.gm.forward(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\n            result = self.candidate(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\n\n            # TODO: replace `same` function with the one in testing\n            if same(correct, result):\n                return self.candidate\n\n            raise RuntimeError(f\"incorrect results of backend {self}\")\n\n        except Exception:\n            log.exception(\"error in verify_correctness\")\n            raise\n        finally:\n            self.restore()\n\n\nScope = dict[str, object]\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass OutputGraphGuardsState:\n    \"\"\"\n    A base class containing fields that are considered \"persistent\" when we\n    want to save all the important state for reconstrucing guards in a different\n    process. Normally we don't need to add states here, but we may have to when\n    the information is needed to serialize the guards, so the fields here are\n    supposed to be serializable as a requirement.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    local_scope: Scope\n    global_scope: Scope\n    # f_globals[\"__builtins__\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\n    # implementation detail -\n    # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\n\n    # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\n    # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\n    # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\n\n    # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\n    # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\n    # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\n    # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\n\n    f_builtins = global_scope[\"__builtins__\"]\n    if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n        f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n    return f_builtins\n\n\nclass OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):\n    \"\"\"\n    Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\n    generated fx.Graph.\n\n    OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\n    with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\n    we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\n    the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    side_effects: SideEffects\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        code_options: dict[str, Any],\n        compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\n        root_tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n        export: bool,\n        export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\n        frame_state: Any,\n        local_scope: Scope,\n        global_scope: Scope,\n        f_code: CodeType,\n        torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\n        package: Optional[\"CompilePackage\"],\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\n            local_scope,\n            global_scope,\n            torch_function_mode_stack,\n            guard_on_key_order=set(),\n            input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\n            dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\n            functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\n            current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\n            # It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\n        # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\n        # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\n        # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\n        self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\n\n        shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n            # Reference Cycle!\n            # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\n            #\n            # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\n            # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\n            # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\n            # the program execution.\n            tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\n            allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\n            allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\n            prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\n            allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\n            co_fields=self.co_fields,\n        )\n\n        # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\n        # of the user marked dynamic dims\n        import torch._functorch.config as _config\n\n        with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\n            fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n                shape_env=shape_env,\n                # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\n                allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\n                export=self.export,\n            )\n        self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n        self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\n        self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\n            CompileContext.current_compile_id()\n        )\n        self.init_ambient_guards()\n\n        # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\n        # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\n        # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\n        # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\n        # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\n        self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\n            collections.defaultdict(list)\n        )\n        # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\n        self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\n        self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\n        # Cached variable trackers. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\n        # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\n        # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\n        # back for backend errors.\n        self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\n\n        # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n        # This information is useful for logging.\n        self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n\n        # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n        # This information is useful for logging.\n        self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n\n        # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\n        # breaks. presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\n        self.save_global_state()\n\n        # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\n        # i.e. buffers and parameters.\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\n\n        # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\n        # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\n        # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\n        # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\n        self.random_calls: list[\n            tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\n        ] = []\n        self.random_values_var: Any = None\n\n        # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\n        self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\n\n        # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\n        self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\n        self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\n        self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\n\n        self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\n            self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\n        )\n\n        self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n\n        # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\n        # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\n        # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\n        # context manager inside a compiled region\n        self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\n            self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\n        )\n\n    def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\n        self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\n            dynamo_timed(\n                \"bytecode_tracing\",\n                log_pt2_compile_event=True,\n            )\n        )\n\n    def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\n        self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\n\n    def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\n        f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\n        return self.install_global(\"__builtins_dict__\", f_builtins)\n\n    def add_backward_state_hook(\n        self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \"hook\"\n    ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\n        name = f\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\"\n        assert name not in self.backward_state\n        self.backward_state[name] = hook\n        return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\n\n    def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n        if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\n            if self.export:\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"backward_state does not support export\",\n                    context=\"\",\n                    explanation=\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\",\n                    hints=[],\n                )\n            example_value = BackwardState()\n            self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(\n                \"dynamo_backward_state\",\n                type(example_value),\n                example_value,\n                source=BackwardStateSource(),\n            )\n            self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta[\"grapharg\"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()\n            self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()\n        return self.backward_state_proxy\n\n    # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative\n    def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:\n        # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards\n        # that show up in ShapeEnv\n        self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))\n\n        self.guards.add(\n            GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)\n        )\n\n        self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))\n\n        self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))\n\n        self.guards.add(\n            GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)\n        )\n\n        ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()\n        if ci is not None:\n            self.guards.add(\n                GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)\n            )\n        if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n            self.guards.add(\n                GlobalStateSource().make_guard(\n                    GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS\n                )\n            )\n\n    def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:\n        if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n            return None\n\n        get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks\n        are_inline_hooks = (\n            torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.saved_tensors_hooks_are_inlineable\n        )\n        hooks = get_hooks()\n        if not are_inline_hooks(hooks):\n            return None\n\n        # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,\n        # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.\n        # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.\n\n        pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks\n        pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n            \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack\",\n            torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),\n        )\n        unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n            \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack\",\n            torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),\n        )\n        assert pack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack_0\"\n        assert unpack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack_0\"\n        return [pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]\n\n    def dump_guards_state(self) -> OutputGraphGuardsState:\n        # Dump a serializable version of self without extras\n        return OutputGraphGuardsState(\n            local_scope=self.local_scope,\n            global_scope=self.global_scope,\n            torch_function_mode_stack=self.torch_function_mode_stack,\n            guard_on_key_order=self.guard_on_key_order,\n            input_source_to_sizes_strides=self.input_source_to_sizes_strides,\n            dual_level=self.dual_level,\n            functorch_layers=self.functorch_layers,\n            current_device=self.current_device,\n            global_state_guard=self.global_state_guard,\n            name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals=self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals,\n            export=self.export,\n            export_constraints=self.export_constraints,\n            _guards=self.guards,\n            _aotautograd_guards=self.aotautograd_guards,\n        )\n\n    def synthetic_graph_input(\n        self, fn: Callable[..., Any], args: tuple[Any, ...]\n    ) -> VariableTracker:\n        \"\"\"\n        call fn(*args) before the graph runs and turn the result into a fake input.\n        \"\"\"\n        example_value = fn(*args)\n        varname = self.new_var()\n        cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\n        cg.add_push_null(\n            lambda: cg.load_import_from(\n                fn.__module__,\n                fn.__name__,\n            )\n        )\n        cg.foreach(map(variables.ConstantVariable.create, args))\n        cg.call_function(len(args), False)\n        cg.store(varname)\n        self.pregraph_bytecode.extend(cg.get_instructions())\n        source = SyntheticLocalSource(varname)\n        result = VariableTracker.build(self.root_tx, example_value, source)\n        # Realize the VT because we will delete the guards on it in the next line.\n        result = result.realize()\n        TracingContext.get().guards_context.dynamo_guards.remove_guards_with_source(\n            source\n        )\n        return result\n\n    def add_cleanup_hook(self, fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> None:\n        self.cleanup_hooks.append(fn)\n\n    def call_cleanup_hooks(self) -> None:\n        for hook in reversed(self.cleanup_hooks):\n            hook()\n        self.cleanup_hooks.clear()\n\n    @property\n    def root_tracer(self) -> \"SubgraphTracer\":\n        return self.tracers[0]\n\n    @property\n    def current_tracer(self) -> \"SubgraphTracer\":\n        return self.tracers[-1]\n\n    def is_root_tracer(self) -> bool:\n        # Helper to tell if we are inside the higher order operator tracing.\n        return len(self.tracers) == 1\n\n    @property\n    def graph(self) -> torch.fx.Graph:\n        return self.current_tracer.graph\n\n    # TODO(rzou): can delete after we refactor speculate_subgraph to use nested GraphTracer.\n    @graph.setter\n    def graph(self, value: torch.fx.Graph) -> None:\n        self.current_tracer.graph = value\n\n    @property\n    def input_name_to_proxy(self) -> dict[str, fx.Proxy]:\n        return self.current_tracer.input_name_to_proxy\n\n    @property\n    def real_value_cache(self) -> dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor]:\n        return self.current_tracer.real_value_cache\n\n    @property\n    def bound_symbols(self) -> dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, \"LazyProxy\"]]:\n        return self.current_tracer.bound_symbols\n\n    # If you are here, and you're looking for create_graph_input,\n    # to avoid ambiguity, please call one of the following:\n    # - self.current_tracer.create_graph_input\n    # - self.root_tracer.create_graph_input\n    # See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] for more context.\n\n    def create_proxy(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n        return self.current_tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    def create_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Node:\n        return self.current_tracer.create_node(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    def remove_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:\n        return self.current_tracer.remove_node(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    @contextlib.contextmanager\n    def subtracer(\n        self, source_target: Optional[Target], prior_tracer: \"SubgraphTracer\"\n    ) -> Generator[fx.Tracer, None, None]:\n        new_scope_ctx = enter_new_scope()\n        try:\n            if prior_tracer:\n                # Lineage MUST stay preserved\n                assert prior_tracer.parent is self.current_tracer\n            new_scope_ctx.__enter__()\n            tracer = (\n                prior_tracer\n                if prior_tracer\n                else SubgraphTracer(\n                    self,\n                    parent=self.current_tracer,\n                    source_target=source_target,\n                    is_export=self.current_tracer.is_export,\n                )\n            )\n            self.tracers.append(tracer)\n            yield tracer\n        finally:\n            new_scope_ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n            self.tracers.pop()\n\n    @property\n    def output(self) -> \"OutputGraph\":\n        return self\n\n    @property\n    def fake_mode(self) -> torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode:\n        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n        return self.tracing_context.fake_mode\n\n    @property\n    def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\n        return self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env\n\n    @property\n    def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\n        return self.tracing_context.guards_context.dynamo_guards\n\n    @property\n    def nn_modules(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return self.tracing_context.module_context.nn_modules\n\n    @property\n    def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\n        return self.tracing_context.guards_context.aotautograd_guards\n\n    def save_global_state(\n        self, out: Optional[dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]]] = None\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Saves to out if it is provided.
